<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 3+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Unifying Emblem](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2223)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Tattoo" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=262" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 123

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** tattooed on the body

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

These tattoos were first created using designs and techniques from the seven Shoanti clans. Each clan is known for one tattoo in particular. The clans would seal alliances in ancient days by tattooing their emblems on members of other clans to symbolically share their gifts. Though these tattoos are respected, the clans reserve their most prestigious symbols for true members of the clan.

The tattoo allows you to understand and speak [Shoanti](/Languages.aspx?ID=171). If you already know that language, you instead gain a +1 item bonus on [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) checks you make when speaking Shoanti to someone who understands it.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> The actions required to Activate the tattoo are the same as those needed to cast its spell;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** The tattoo casts its spell.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

item_bonus_note: Stealth check bonus increases in forests
markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 4+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Wildwood Ink](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2224)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Primal" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=134" />
<trait label="Tattoo" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=262" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 123

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** tattooed on the body

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

These curving, delicate designs resemble leaves, vines, or creepers, most often wrapped around a limb, ear, or throat, or curled around specific muscles. They help you blend in among plants. You gain a +1 item bonus to [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) checks, which increases to +2 in forests.

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** A creature would detect you by [Seeking](/Actions.aspx?ID=84); **Requirements** You're in a forest or similar natural area; **Effect** The tattoo casts [_one with plants_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1618) to turn you into a plant before you can be noticed. The duration of this spell is 10 minutes. 

If you’ve already Activated the tattoo, you can supply a separate casting of _one with plants_ to recharge the tattoo instead of having the spell’s normal effect. This allows you to Activate the tattoo again in the same day. You can do so multiple times each day, but only as many times as you continue to cast one with plants to recharge the tattoo after each use.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

level="2"
right="Item 4"
pfs="Standard"
>
Wildwood Ink
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 123

**Price** 80 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 10"
pfs="Standard"
>
Wildwood Ink (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 123

**Price** 900 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The item bonus to Stealth checks is +2, or +3 in forests. _One with plants_ lasts up to 1 hour.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 17"
pfs="Standard"
>
Wildwood Ink (Major)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 123

**Price** 15,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The item bonus to Stealth checks is +3, or +4 in forests. _One with plants_ lasts up to 8 hours.
name: Wildwood Ink

item_bonus_note: Item bonus to Athletic checks to Trip
markdown:
**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 124 **Usage** affixed to armor or a weapon --- This silver disc displays an ever-changing etching of an [animal](/Traits.aspx?ID=9). When you affix the _beastmaster's sigil_, the animal settles into a single form based on where you affix it, showing the animal the item can summon when affixed that way. The spell DC of any spell cast by activating this item is 19. A weapon can benefit from only the melee weapon benefit or ranged weapon benefit. A [thrown](/Traits.aspx?ID=195) melee weapon gets the melee weapon benefit, and a [combination](/Traits.aspx?ID=417) weapon gets only one benefit, which you choose when you affix the spellheart. If it's unclear which one should apply, the GM decides.
  • **Armor** The sigil grants you a +1 item bonus to saving throws against [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126).
  • **Melee Weapon** The sigil grants you a +1 item bonus to [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) checks to [Trip](/Actions.aspx?ID=40).
  • **Ranged Weapon** If you critically hit with the affixed weapon, and the target is adjacent to a creature summoned with the sigil, the target takes 1d6 [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29).
**Activate** Cast a Spell; **Effect** You cast [_tame_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1014). Beastmaster's Sigil **Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 124 **Price** 160 gp --- **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You cast 2nd-rank [_summon animal_](/Spells.aspx?ID=316) to summon a [hunting spider](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3207) (armor), [wolf](/Monsters.aspx?ID=415) (melee weapon), or [bat swarm](/Monsters.aspx?ID=45) (ranged weapon). Beastmaster's Sigil (Greater) **Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 124 **Price** 360 gp --- The spell DC is 23 and the bleed damage is 2d6. **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You cast 3rd-rank _summon animal_ to summon a [giant monitor lizard](/Monsters.aspx?ID=289) (armor), [boar](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2854) (melee), or [giant bat](/Monsters.aspx?ID=46) (ranged). Beastmaster's Sigil (Major) **Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 124 **Price** 700 gp --- The spell DC is 25, the item bonuses are +2, and the bleed damage is 3d6. **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You cast 4th-rank _summon animal_ to summon a [giant scorpion](/Monsters.aspx?ID=356) (armor), [dire wolf](/Monsters.aspx?ID=416) (melee), or [fen mosquito swarm](/Monsters.aspx?ID=732) (ranged).
name: Beastmaster's Sigil

item_bonus_note: Thievery checks to Pick a Lock
markdown:
**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 127 _[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) The standard version is permitted for use while the greater and major versions are limited._ **Usage** affixed to armor or a weapon --- This ornate doorknob can open doors both material and metaphysical, revealing dreams and nightmares. The spell DC of any spell cast by activating this item is 20.
  • **Armor** You gain a +1 item bonus to [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=17) checks to [Pick a Lock](/Actions.aspx?ID=70).
  • **Weapon** If you critically succeed at a Strike with the weapon, the target is [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=7) until the end of its next turn.
**Activate** Cast a Spell; **Effect** You cast [_figment_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1528). Phantasmal Doorknob **Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 127 **Price** 215 gp --- **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You cast [_phantasmal treasure_](/Spells.aspx?ID=707). Phantasmal Doorknob (Greater) **Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 127 **Price** 900 gp --- The item bonus when affixed to armor is +2, and the spell DC is 27. On a critical hit with a weapon the doorknob is affixed to, the target is [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=1) until the end of its next turn. The creature is then temporarily immune to being blinded this way for 24 hours. **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You cast [_vision of death_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1742). Phantasmal Doorknob (Major) **Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 127 **Price** 6,000 gp --- The item bonus when affixed to armor is +2, and the spell DC is 34. On a critical hit with a weapon the doorknob is affixed to, the target is blinded until the end of its next turn. The creature is then temporarily immune to being blinded this way for 24 hours. **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You cast 6th-rank _vision of death_. **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You cast [_phantasmal calamity_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1630).
name: Phantasmal Doorknob

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 136 **Usage** held in 2 hands **Bulk** 1 --- While those who appreciate bagpipes may like the sound of this gray reed and black leather instrument, its real purpose is to sow turmoil against a performer's enemies, spreading discord with each note. While playing the bagpipes, you gain a +1 item bonus to [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12) checks and to [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) checks made to [Demoralize](/Actions.aspx?ID=53). **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Effect** You expend a number of charges from this instrument to cast a spell from its list. **Craft Requirements** Supply one casting of all listed levels of all listed spells. Bagpipes of Turmoil **Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 136 **Price** 90 gp **Bulk** 1 ---
  • **Cantrip** [_daze_](/Spells.aspx?ID=61)
  • **1st** [_bane_](/Spells.aspx?ID=18)
Bagpipes of Turmoil (Greater) **Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 136 **Price** 460 gp **Bulk** 1 ---
  • **2nd** [_deafness_](/Spells.aspx?ID=62), [_knock_](/Spells.aspx?ID=168), [_shatter_](/Spells.aspx?ID=279)
  • **3rd** [_paralyze_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1622), [_shift blame_](/Spells.aspx?ID=997)
Bagpipes of Turmoil (Major) **Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 136 **Price** 1,900 gp **Bulk** 1 --- The item bonuses are +2.
  • **4th** [_confusion_](/Spells.aspx?ID=48), [_painful vibrations_](/Spells.aspx?ID=964)
  • **5th** [_banishment_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1448), [_synaptic pulse_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1710), [_wave of despair_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1757)
name: Bagpipes of Turmoil

markdown:
**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 136 **Usage** held in 2 hands **Bulk** 1 --- This handheld snare drum is adorned with garish scenes of battle and triumph. When played, no matter what rhythm, it always gives the impression of a marching beat, invoking armies on the move. While playing the drums, you gain a +1 item bonus to [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12) checks and a +5-foot status bonus to your Speed. **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Effect** You expend a number of charges from this instrument to cast a spell from its list. **Craft Requirements** Supply one casting of all listed levels of all listed spells. Drums of War **Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 136 **Price** 90 gp **Bulk** 1 ---
  • **Cantrip** [_shield_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1671)
  • **1st** [_force barrage_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1536), [_mystic armor_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1611), [_sure strike_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1709)
Drums of War (Greater) **Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 136 **Price** 460 gp **Bulk** 1 ---
  • **2nd** [_false vitality_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1523), [_noise blast_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1616), [_spiritual armament_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1687)
  • **3rd** [_haste_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1553), [_heroism_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1555)
Drums of War (Major) **Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 136 **Price** 1,900 gp **Bulk** 1 --- The item bonus to Performance checks is +2.
  • **4th** [_dispelling globe_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1494), [_tortoise and the hare_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1021)
  • **5th** _dispelling globe_, [_quicken time_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1187), [_synaptic pulse_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1710)
name: Drums of War

markdown:
**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 136 **Usage** held in 2 hands **Bulk** L --- This lustrous lute has a polished body that changes to show whatever decorations or carvings you find most appealing, or which best reinforce the story of the song you're currently playing (as a free action). Its frets are inlaid with mother of pearl. With its mix of enchantment and illusion magic, it's favored by many traveling minstrels. While playing the lute, you gain a +1 item bonus to [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) and [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12) checks. **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Effect** You expend a number of charges from this instrument to cast a spell from its list. **Craft Requirements** Supply one casting of all listed levels of all listed spells. Entertainer's Lute **Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 136 **Price** 90 gp **Bulk** L ---
  • **Cantrip** [_infectious enthusiasm_](/Spells.aspx?ID=932)
  • **1st** [_bless_](/Spells.aspx?ID=25), [_ventriloquism_](/Spells.aspx?ID=356)
Entertainer's Lute (Greater) **Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 136 **Price** 460 gp **Bulk** L ---
  • **2nd** [_calm_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1458), [_phantasmal treasure_](/Spells.aspx?ID=2013)
  • **3rd** [_enthrall_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1516), [_heroism_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1555), [_illusory creature_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1567)
Entertainer's Lute (Major) **Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 136 **Price** 1,900 gp **Bulk** L --- The item bonuses are +2.
  • **4th** [_honeyed words_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1558), [_infectious melody_](/Spells.aspx?ID=933)
  • **5th** [_hallucination_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1551), [_illusory creature_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1567), [_illusory scene_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1570), [_suggestion_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1693)
name: Entertainer's Lute

markdown:
**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 136 **Price** 10,000 gp **Usage** held in 2 hands **Bulk** L --- This exquisite fiddle is perfectly carved and balanced to produce the purest sound while granting its player perfect balance and poise. It grants a +2 item bonus to [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) checks. If you’re a master in Performance, while playing it you also gain a +1 status bonus to Reflex saves and a +1 item bonus to Dexterity-based skill checks. If you’re legendary in Performance, the bonuses are +2. When you [Perform](/Actions.aspx?ID=2401) with the fiddle, you can choose to create a harsh and discordant note. You critically fail the Performance check, shred the strings of the fiddle, and create an 8th-rank [_noise blast_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1616), with a 40-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) around you instead of a 10-foot [burst](/Rules.aspx?ID=2385). The fiddle can’t be played again until the strings are replaced at a cost of 200 gp. **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Effect** You expend a number of charges from this instrument to cast a spell from its list.
  • **Cantrip** [_figment_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1528), [_light_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1585), [_message_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1598)
  • **1st** [_command_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1470)
  • **2nd** [_calm_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1458), [_revealing light_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1653)
  • **3rd** [_enthrall_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1516)
  • **4th** [_honeyed words_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1558)
  • **5th** [_sending_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1665)
  • **6th** _calm_, [_dominate_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1501), [_vibrant pattern_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1741)
  • **7th** _dominate_, [_mask of terror_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1595), [_true target_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1726), _vibrant pattern_, [_visions of danger_](/Spells.aspx?ID=2040)
**Craft Requirements** Supply one casting of all listed levels of all listed spells.
name: Fiddle of the Maestro

item_bonus_note: while playing the mandolin
markdown:
**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 137 **Usage** held in 2 hands **Bulk** L --- Sought after by many unscrupulous [bards](/Classes.aspx?ID=3), this instrument is surprisingly light and easy to carry, but also empowered with a number of spells carefully selected to help with fooling others or making a hasty retreat. While playing the mandolin, you gain a +1 item bonus to [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) and [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12) checks. **Activate** ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Effect** You change the instrument's color and shape to one you prefer, and you can turn it into a different handheld string instrument that takes two hands to play. **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Effect** You expend a number of charges from this instrument to cast a spell from its list. **Craft Requirements** Supply one casting of all listed levels of all listed spells. Trickster's Mandolin **Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 137 **Price** 90 gp **Bulk** L ---
  • **Cantrip** [_prestidigitation_](/Spells.aspx?ID=229)
  • **1st** [_illusory disguise_](/Spells.aspx?ID=159), [_item facade_](/Spells.aspx?ID=166), [_ventriloquism_](/Spells.aspx?ID=356)
Trickster's Mandolin (Greater) **Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 137 **Price** 460 gp **Bulk** L ---
  • **2nd** [_blur_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1455), [_illusory creature_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1567), [_illusory disguise_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1568), [invisibility](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2790)
  • **3rd** _illusory disguise_, [_phantom prison_](/Spells.aspx?ID=969), [_shared invisibility_](/Spells.aspx?ID=2025)
Trickster's Mandolin (Major) **Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 137 **Price** 1,900 gp **Bulk** L --- The item bonuses are +2.
  • **4th** [_confusion_](/Spells.aspx?ID=48), _invisibility_, _illusory disguise_
  • **5th** _hallucination_, [_illusory scene_](/Spells.aspx?ID=161), _Illusory Disguise_
name: Trickster's Mandolin

item_bonus_note: Crafting checks to create alchemical items
markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 11"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Alchemist's Haversack](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2305)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Extradimensional" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=67" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 145

**Price** 1,400 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn backpack

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

An _alchemist’s haversack_ is a sturdy leather backpack with two compartments. The main section contains an extradimensional space equivalent to a [spacious pouch type II](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3032), perfect for carrying bulkier alchemist equipment. A secondary partition can hold 2 Bulk of items, 1 of which doesn’t count against your Bulk limit. This second compartment can also be activated (see below).

Two shoulder straps and a thick belt for the waist hold the haversack on the wearer securely. These straps have pouches and loops for keeping alchemical items handy. They also contain magically [expanded alchemist’s toolkit](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2702) that grants you a +2 item bonus to [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37) checks to create alchemical items.

Water and undesirable material can’t seep into the haversack, which cleans itself once per hour. When you reach in any part of the haversack, the item you sought is the first one you find. Also, the haversack preserves mundane ingredients, food, and drink inside, so they stay fresh indefinitely. This feature doesn’t prolong the duration of magic or alchemical items.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day;  **Requirements** You can create versatile vials; **Effect** You pull a versatile vial from the satchel’s secondary compartment. It’s as effective as the vials you create at the start of the day, not limited like ones created using [Quick Alchemy](/Actions.aspx?ID=2801).

**Craft Requirements** You are an [alchemist](/Classes.aspx?ID=1).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Alchemist's Haversack

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 6"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Vaultbreaker's Harness](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2307)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 145, [Pathfinder #158: Sixty Feet Under](/Sources.aspx?ID=47) pg. 76

**Price** 230 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn backpack

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A _vaultbreaker's harness_ has four pockets across the chest. The pockets contain a set of [infiltrator's thieves' tools](/Equipment.aspx?ID=58), infiltrator picks, a [levered crowbar](/Equipment.aspx?ID=18), and a [glass cutter](/Equipment.aspx?ID=870). These items are magically bound to the harness; if they are more than 1 foot away from you, they disappear, then reappear in the harness at the next sunset. [Broken](/Conditions.aspx?ID=2) or destroyed items similarly reappear, restored, in their proper pockets at sunset.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645))    **Effect** You cinch the harness to prepare for mischief. You gain a +1 item bonus to [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) checks and a +10-foot item bonus to your Speed for 1 minute.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Vaultbreaker's Harness

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 18"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Clockwork Cloak](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2310)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Clockwork" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=331" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 146, [Pathfinder #161: Belly of the Black Whale](/Sources.aspx?ID=51) pg. 79

**Price** 20,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn cloak

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Paper-thin interlocking cogs and gears make up the bronze _clockwork cloak_.

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** You're struck by a melee attack with a held weapon;  **Effect** The folds of the cloak attempt to divert the attack and catch the weapon in the cloak's gears. Make an [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) check to [Disarm](/Actions.aspx?ID=41) the attacking creature.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645))    **Effect** You wrap the cloak around yourself and the winding gears decelerate your body, causing you to enter standby mode. While in standby mode you don't need to eat, drink, or sleep. You remain aware of your surroundings but take a –4 penalty to Perception checks. You can stay in standby mode indefinitely, although your body ages normally. You can leave standby mode as a free action. If you do so to initiate combat, you gain a +2 item bonus to your initiative roll.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Clockwork Cloak

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 8+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Clarity Goggles](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2314)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 146

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) The counteract check from a pair of clarity goggles has a counteract rank of half the item’s level rounded up._

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn eyepiece

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

_Clarity goggles_ feature faceted lenses that filter your surroundings from several slightly different angles at once, giving you a sharper picture of them. While wearing the goggles, you gain a +1 item bonus to visual Perception checks.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You focus on your environment and the creatures around you to see them as they really are. The GM rolls a [secret](/Traits.aspx?ID=142) [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) check using your Perception bonus against any [illusion](/Traits.aspx?ID=92) effect created by a 3rd-rank or lower spell or a creature of 8th level or lower. You must be able to see the illusion, and it must be within 60 feet. If the check succeeds, you see through the illusion for 10 minutes.

The creature must attempt a DC 20 Fortitude save as it becomes disoriented by this reflection. On a failure, the creature is [sickened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34) (sickened 2 on a critical failure). The creature is temporarily immune for 1 hour.

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 12"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Spectacles of Inquiry](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2316)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 147

**Price** 1,750 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn eyepiece

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Anything viewed through these thin spectacles looks crisp and clear, and the earpieces accentuate sounds around you. You gain a +2 item bonus to Perception checks.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per hour;   **Effect** The spectacles key in on someone to show you their social cues in perfect clarity. Choose a creature you can see. You gain a +3 item bonus on Perception checks you make to use [Sense Motive](/Actions.aspx?ID=85) against that creature. This benefit lasts until you Activate the item again in this way.

 If you’re an [investigator](/Classes.aspx?ID=59), you can use this activation as part of the [Devise a Stratagem](/Actions.aspx?ID=2813) action (even if you’re doing it as a free action). Alternatively, you can use it as part of the [Pursue a Lead](/Actions.aspx?ID=2811) activity, even though you can’t see the creature. You must choose the same creature you chose for Devise a Stratagem or Pursue a Lead.

**Craft Requirements** You are an investigator.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Spectacles of Inquiry

</title>

<traits>

<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn eyepiece

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

With lenses set in a silver frame, _spectacles of piercing_ sight grant you a +3 item bonus to visual Perception checks.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per hour;   **Effect** You can see into and through solid matter for 1 minute. This vision can pierce through solid materials up to 20 feet away as if looking at something in normal light even if no illumination is available. You can see through up to 1 foot of stone, 1 inch of metal, or 3 feet of wood or dirt. A thin sheet of lead blocks this vision entirely.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 11"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Accompaniment Cloak](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2318)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Focused" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=74" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Occult" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=120" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 147

**Price** 1,300 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn cloak

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This lush velvet capelet is embroidered with images of musicians playing a wide variety of instruments. The images animate when you make art, remaining embroidered, but smoothly unknitting and reknitting in time with your performance. The figures play music that perfectly accompanies your instrument, voice, or movements, granting you a +2 item bonus to [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12) checks.

**Activate** <actions string="Free Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You gain 1 Focus Point, which you can use only to cast a bard [composition](/Traits.aspx?ID=31) spell. If not used by the end of your turn, this Focus Point is lost.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You peel the musicians from the garment and fling them around you. The cloak casts a 4th-rank [_phantom crowd_](/Spells.aspx?ID=968) spell (DC 28); each of the 10-foot squares must be adjacent to you. The crowd looks like the musicians on the garment and continues to accompany your Performance checks. You can Sustain this effect as described in the spell.

**Craft Requirements** You are a [bard](/Classes.aspx?ID=3).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Accompaniment Cloak

---

Each _bloodline robe_ has a design that befits a particular [sorcerer](/Classes.aspx?ID=11) bloodline, depicting creatures of that bloodline or using styles common among them. You gain a +2 item bonus to each of your bloodline skills.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day;  **Requirements** You're benefiting from your blood magic effect; **Effect** You're [quickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=32) on your next turn. You can use the extra action only as part of Casting a bloodline spell.

**Activate** <actions string="Free Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You gain 1 Focus Point, which you can use only to cast a sorcerer bloodline spell. If not used by the end of your turn, this Focus Point is lost.

**Craft Requirements** You are a sorcerer of the bloodline tied to the robe.

    right="Item 9+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Cloister Robe](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2321)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Divine" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=48" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 148

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn garment

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Armor** [Explorer's Clothing](/Armor.aspx?ID=2)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

The most devoted, [cloistered clerics](/Doctrines.aspx?ID=2) wear a _cloister robe_. Decorations symbolic of a specific deity adorn the robe, and the robe's colors and the complexity of its construction fit the deity's outlook. The robe serves as a [religious symbol](/Equipment.aspx?ID=41) of that deity, and it doesn't need to be wielded to provide that benefit.

 The robe is _+1 [resilient](/Equipment.aspx?ID=279) [explorer's clothing](/Armor.aspx?ID=2)_ and grants a +1 item bonus to [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) checks. The robe doesn't grant any benefits to a wearer who doesn't worship the deity tied to the robe. In addition, when you cast a domain spell from one of the deity's domains, you gain resistance to damage from [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48) spells until the end of your next turn. This resistance is equal to half the robe's level.

**Craft Requirements** You are a [cleric](/Classes.aspx?ID=5) who worships the deity tied to the robe.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

item_bonus_note: Diplomacy checks while interacting with dragons of the same type
markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 11"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Dragon Rune Bracelet](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2322)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Focused" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=74" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 148, [Pathfinder #171: Hurricane's Howl](/Sources.aspx?ID=101) pg. 75

**Price** 1,400 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A _dragon rune bracelet_ is a gold bangle formed around the scale of a famous [dragon](/Traits.aspx?ID=50). The bracelet is etched with esoteric symbols or words in [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=2) that indicate kinship with dragons. As many types of _dragon rune bracelet_ exist as there are types of dragons, though bracelets associated with [uncommon](/Traits.aspx?ID=159) or [rare](/Traits.aspx?ID=137) dragons have the same rarity as the dragon.

 While wearing a _dragon rune bracelet_, you gain a +2 item bonus to [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) checks while interacting with dragons of the same type as the bracelet's scale (such as [red dragons](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=168)  or [cloud dragons](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=176)). You also gain a +2 item bonus to saving throws against [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68) effects from any kind of dragon.

**Activate** <actions string="Free Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You gain 1 Focus Point, which you can spend only to cast a [sorcerer](/Classes.aspx?ID=11) [draconic bloodline](/Bloodlines.aspx?ID=5) spell. If not used by the end of your turn, this Focus Point is lost.

**Craft Requirements** You are a sorcerer of a draconic bloodline.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Dragon Rune Bracelet

item_bonus_note: The skill that matches your eidolon's tradition
markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 11"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Eidolon Cape](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2323)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Focused" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=74" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 148

**Price** 1,400 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn cloak

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Though it appears to be one elegant piece, an _eidolon cape_ consists of a mantle for your shoulders and a detachable cape. The cape is designed to resemble your [eidolon](Eidolons.aspx), either with a direct likeness or with features reminiscent of your eidolon. For instance, if your eidolon is a [dragon](/Traits.aspx?ID=50), the cape might depict a stylized dragon, or it might have a pattern of colorful scales with gold trim. An eidolon cape features the sigil you share with your eidolon prominently in its design. You gain a +2 item bonus to the skill that matches your eidolon's tradition: [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2) for [arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=11), [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) for [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) for [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120), or [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) for [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134).

 When you [Manifest your Eidolon](/Actions.aspx?ID=757), the cape transforms into the eidolon, and when the eidolon unmanifests, it turns back into the cape. If the cape is attached to the mantle, it comes free immediately, and the eidolon appears adjacent to you as normal. If not, the eidolon appears in a space adjacent to the cape. With the cape detached, the mantle is still light Bulk, and the whole cloak remains invested. If the cape is out of range (beyond 100 feet, typically) or there's not enough open space for the eidolon to manifest, your attempt to Manifest your Eidolon fails.

**Activate** <actions string="Free Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You gain 1 Focus Point, which you can use only to cast a summoner link spell. If not used by the end of your turn, this Focus Point is lost.

**Craft Requirements** You are a [summoner](/Classes.aspx?ID=18).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Eidolon Cape

If the cape is out of range (beyond 100 feet, typically) or there's not enough open space for the eidolon to manifest, your attempt to Manifest your Eidolon fails.

**Activate** <actions string="Free Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You gain 1 Focus Point, which you can use only to cast a summoner link spell. If not used by the end of your turn, this Focus Point is lost.

**Craft Requirements** You are a [summoner](/Classes.aspx?ID=18).

</column>

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 11"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Hexing Jar](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2324)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Focused" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=74" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 148

**Price** 1,200 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Dangling from a simple cord, a _hexing jar_ houses rich soil. When a [witch](/Classes.aspx?ID=16) who has invested the jar wears it overnight, a miniature thing grows from the soil. Your patron chooses the form, commonly including glowing mushrooms, venus flytraps, mandragora roots, or undead hands reaching up. The thing whispers secrets it learned from your patron, giving you a +2 item bonus to your patron skill.

**Activate** <actions string="Free Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You gain 1 Focus Point, which you can use only to cast a witch [hex](/Traits.aspx?ID=324) spell. If not used by the end of your turn, this Focus Point is lost.

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per hour; **Trigger** You roll a critical success on an attack roll with a witch spell, or your target rolls a critical failure on its saving throw against a witch spell or hex you cast;  **Effect** The thing in the jar becomes more energetic—glowing, dancing, rapping on the glass, or some other action appropriate to its appearance. It encourages you until the start of your next turn, granting you and your familiar a +1 status bonus to AC and saving throws and a +2 status bonus against [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106) effects.

**Craft Requirements** You are a witch.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

As you move your body, qi flows into _mala beads of foresight_ you wear and have invested, making them one with your life force. In their usual form, beads are spheres of wood, but versions customized to different martial orders are common. You gain a +2 item bonus to [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) checks.

**Activate** <actions string="Free Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You gain 1 Focus Point, which you can use only to cast a [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48) monk qi spell. If not used by the end of your turn, this Focus Point is lost.

**Activate** <actions string="Free Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per hour; **Trigger** You have just [Refocused](/Actions.aspx?ID=71) by meditating;  **Effect** While meditating, you searched your feelings for a portent of the future. You're affected by an [_augury_](/Spells.aspx?ID=15) spell.

**Craft Requirements** You are a [monk](/Classes.aspx?ID=8) with divine qi spells.

</column>

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 11"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Mask of Uncanny Breath](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2326)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Focused" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=74" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Occult" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=120" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 150

**Price** 1,200 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn mask

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A thin wooden mask carved in the shape of a skull, monstrous face, or eerily featureless visage, a mask of uncanny breath fully covers your face. While wearing it, each breath you take feels cool and pure, perfectly flowing to feed your qi. You gain resistance 10 to [inhaled](/Traits.aspx?ID=96) [poisons](/Traits.aspx?ID=126) and can breathe in an airless or toxic environment. When you breathe in, fragments of bizarre knowledge flow through you, granting you a +2 item bonus to [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) checks.

**Activate** <actions string="Free Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You gain 1 Focus Point, which you can use only to cast an [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120) monk qi spell. If not used by the end of your turn, this Focus Point is lost.

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** Your [unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=199) Strike hits a creature that breathes;  **Effect** The mask contorts and inhales, sucking breath from your target's lungs. The target falls [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38) but doesn't fall [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=31) or drop what it's holding. It wakes up at the end of your turn if it hasn't been woken up already.

**Craft Requirements** You are a [monk](/Classes.aspx?ID=8) with occult qi spells.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

>
[Master Magus Ring](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2327)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Arcane" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=11" />
<trait label="Focused" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=74" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 150

**Price** 1,250 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Elegant jewelry such as the _master magus ring_ adorns experienced [magi](/Classes.aspx?ID=17). Each ring has a significant metal and symbol to represent a particular hybrid study, such as a heavy iron ring with an icon of a mountain for [inexorable iron](/HybridStudies.aspx?ID=1), or glittering silver with a shield-like emblem for [sparkling targe](/HybridStudies.aspx?ID=3). You gain a +2 item bonus to [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2) checks.

**Activate** <actions string="Free Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You gain 1 Focus Point, which you can use only to cast a magus conflux spell. If not used by the end of your turn, this Focus Point is lost.

**Activate** <actions string="Free Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** The ring transports you and any items you're wearing and holding from your current space to an unoccupied space you can see within a range equal to your Speed. If this would bring another creature with you—even if you're carrying it in an [extradimensional](/Traits.aspx?ID=67) container—the activation fails and is used.

**Craft Requirements** You are a magus.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 11"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Mind's Light Circlet](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2328)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Focused" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=74" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Light" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=100" />
<trait label="Occult" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=120" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 150

**Price** 1,200 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn headwear

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Gemstones of many colors adorn the silver of a _mind's light circlet_. When you're charged with mental power, the jewels scintillate with light, with different gems resonating based on your emotions. If you have at least 1 Focus Point, the gems cast [dim light](/Rules.aspx?ID=408) in a 10-foot radius. When you amp a spell, the light increases to [bright light](/Rules.aspx?ID=407) in a 20-foot radius (and dim light to the next 20 feet) until the start of your next turn.

 You gain a +2 item bonus to [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) checks. You also gain the following [amp](/Traits.aspx?ID=425), which you can apply to any of your psi cantrips that have a target or area, much like an amp gained from a feat.

 **Amp** You transfer some of the magical luminescence from the mind's light circlet to one of the creatures. Choose a creature targeted by the spell or in its area. Until the start of your next turn, that creature sheds bright light in a 20-foot radius (and dim light to the next 20 feet) and can't be [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4). If the creature is [invisible](/Conditions.aspx?ID=26), it's concealed while alight, rather than being [undetected](/Conditions.aspx?ID=39).

**Activate** <actions string="Free Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You gain 1 Focus Point, which you can use only to use a psychic amp. If not used by the end of your turn, this Focus Point is lost.

**Craft Requirements** You are a [psychic](/Classes.aspx?ID=21).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Mind's Light Circlet

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 11"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Oracular Crown](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2329)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Divine" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=48" />
<trait label="Focused" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=74" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 151

**Price** 1,200 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn headwear

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Patterns themed to your curse cover your _oracular crown_. As your curse worsens, the appearance of the crown changes, introducing extreme angles, stronger colors, or other indications of the intensity of your curse. Similarly, it gets closer to its natural form when you reduce the effects of your curse. You gain a +2 item bonus to [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) checks.

**Activate** <actions string="Free Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You gain 1 Focus Point, which you can use only to cast an oracle revelation spell. If not used by the end of your turn, this Focus Point is lost.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=623), [vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509)) **Frequency** once per day;  **Requirements** Your [cursebound](/Traits.aspx?ID=800) value is 1 or higher; **Effect** You regain 3d8 Hit Points. The amount of healing increases to 5d8 if you’re cursebound 2, 7d8 if you’re cursebound 3, or 9d8 if you’re cursebound 4. If you have the [void healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=83) ability, this activation has the [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510) trait instead of the healing and vitality traits.

**Craft Requirements** You are an [oracle](/Classes.aspx?ID=14).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Oracular Crown

</title>

<traits>

<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />

</row>

---

This symbol shows your dedication to the magic practiced by some [rangers](/Classes.aspx?ID=9). Most rangers wear it on an amulet, ring, or piercing. You gain a +2 item bonus to [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) checks.

**Activate** <actions string="Free Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You gain 1 Focus Point, which you can use only to cast a ranger warden spell. When you use this Focus Point, the _warden’s signet_ also casts a 4th-rank [_oaken resilience_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1617) spell on you. If not used by the end of your turn, this Focus Point is lost.

**Craft Requirements** You are a ranger with at least one warden spell.

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Sonic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=147" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 151

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn shoes

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Unassuming in appearance, these slippers indicate their nature only with a signature strip of yellow stitching. You gain a +2 item bonus to [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) checks.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You move like the wind, with precision and speed. You Stride up to 60 feet; this movement doesn't trigger reactions. When you stop, if you've moved at least 30 feet from where you started, you release a thunderous 5-foot emanation that deals 2d6 bludgeoning damage and 2d6 sonic damage with a DC 25 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Fortitude save. A creature that critically fails its save is also knocked [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=31).

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 6"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Crown of the Companion](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2334)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Healing" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=89" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Vitality" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=509" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 151, [Pathfinder #170: Spoken on the Song Wind](/Sources.aspx?ID=97) pg. 75

**Price** 250 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn headwear

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Stories tell of a king who once loved his subjects so much he was willing to give his own life energy for them, using an object like the _crown of the companion_. Whether true or not, this majestic wooden crown bears elaborate carvings depicting that tale with images of a regal figure giving increasingly of themself to a throng of needy subjects. While wearing this crown, you gain a +1 item bonus to [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) checks.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You bow to an ally within 30 feet, creating a magical bond between the two of you as if you cast [_share life_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1669) targeting the ally. The link remains even if you move more than 30 feet away from them. At the end of the spell’s duration, your ally recovers 4d8 Hit Points and you recover half of what they recover.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Crown of the Companion

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 151 **Price** 220 gp **Usage** worn headwear **Bulk** L --- Made of materials scavenged from wild places, a _druid's crown_ can be rebuilt for a variety of benefits. The crown grants you a +1 item bonus to a skill, and can be activated to cast a spell, both depending on the material used to build the crown, as listed on the table below. If you invest and wear [_living mantle_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3092) along with the crown, the crown's item bonus increases by 1 and its spell's DC rises to 27. You can invest this item only if you're a [druid](/Classes.aspx?ID=6). When you do, as a 10-minute activity that has the [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104) trait, you can disassemble and rebuild the crown with different materials, changing its item bonus and spell accordingly. ##
**Material****Item Bonus****Spell**
Antlers[Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7)[_Enlarge_](/Spells.aspx?ID=102)
Flowers[Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6)[_Animal allies_](/Spells.aspx?ID=863)
Leaves[Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15)[_One with Plants_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1618) <%END>
 **Activate** ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** The crown casts its spell at 2nd rank (DC 20). **Craft Requirements** You are a druid.
name: Druid's Crown

**Activate** <actions string="Three Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You can breathe underwater for 8 hours. During this time, you have a swim Speed equal to your land Speed.

markdown:
**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 152 **Price** 160 gp **Usage** worn headwear **Bulk** L --- Repurposing and enchanting a helmet worn by a battlefield commander can create a _tactician’s helm_, imparting knowledge of battlefield tactics that feeds off your minor victories. The helm grants you a +1 item bonus to [Warfare Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) checks. Also, a jewel adorns the brow of the helmet. This jewel becomes charged each time you hit a creature with a [Reactive Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2256). A _tactician’s helm_ can hold up to 2 charges, and its charges reset to 0 when you invest it. **Activate** ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561))**Cost** 1 charge from the helm; **Frequency** once per hour; **Effect** You choose one of the following effects.
  • **Charge!** Stride twice.
  • **Move It!** You gain a +2 status bonus to Acrobatics and Athletics checks until the end of this turn.
  • **Protect!** If you're wielding a shield, Stride to a space adjacent to an ally, then Raise your Shield.
  • **Re-Arm!** Interact up to three times. Each of these actions must be used to do something listed under [Interact](/Rules.aspx?ID=2151).
name: Tactician's Helm

[Hunter's Arrowhead](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2343)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />

---

A _hunter's arrowhead_ is meant to be worn as a charm, such as a pendant, or carried in a pocket or quiver. The arrowhead is etched with images sacred to the elven god [Ketephys](/Deities.aspx?ID=110). While you wear or carry the arrowhead, it infuses you with great skill at hunting, and you gain a +1 item bonus to [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) checks and attack rolls against any creature you've currently designated as your prey with [Hunt Prey](/Actions.aspx?ID=10). A _hunter's arrowhead_ is also a [religious symbol](/Equipment.aspx?ID=41) of Ketephys.

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** You would miss with an attack made with a [bow](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=3);  **Effect** You gain a +2 circumstance bonus to your attack roll, possibly turning a miss into a hit.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Hunter's Arrowhead

While invested, the ring grants a +2 item bonus to saving throws while you are in an urban setting, and this increases to a +3 item bonus if you have legendary proficiency in Guild [Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8), Katapesh Lore, Mercantile Lore, or [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14). >
[Spiritsight Ring](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2346)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

The opal set in the intricately carved ivory _spiritsight ring_ eventually becomes translucent and tickles your finger whenever an [incorporeal](/Traits.aspx?ID=222) creature is nearby. When in the presence of a nearby incorporeal creature, even if it's within a solid object, you eventually detect the creature, though you might not do so instantly, and you can't pinpoint the location. This acts as a [vague sense](/Rules.aspx?ID=413), like humans' sense of smell. An incorporeal creature trying to hide its presence from this sense attempts a [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) check against your Perception DC to hide from your vague sense, as normal for attempting to foil [special senses](/Rules.aspx?ID=414). You gain a +2 item bonus when using the [Seek](/Actions.aspx?ID=84) action to find [hidden](/Conditions.aspx?ID=22) or [undetected](/Conditions.aspx?ID=39) incorporeal creatures within 30 feet of you.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Spiritsight Ring

A patchwork of [humanoid](/Traits.aspx?ID=628) flesh makes up a _skinsaw mask_, which is stitched together with black silk or wire. It is distinctive for its bulbous orange eye—crafted from a magical glass bauble—and wide row of teeth. When worn, the mask amplifies your ability to sense fear in other creatures. You know the value of the [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) condition of any observed creature, and you gain a +1 item bonus to [Perception](/Rules.aspx?ID=2298) checks to [Seek](/Actions.aspx?ID=2301) frightened creatures. Whenever you deal precision damage to a frightened creature, you deal 1 additional precision damage. If you aren’t [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521), you are [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68) 2 while wearing a _skinsaw mask_.

item_bonus_note: Stealth checks to Hide and Sneak
markdown:
**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 180 **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** L --- Carved from a scale of the [kaiju](/Traits.aspx?ID=383) Igroon, this jagged shield refracts light around it in a shimmering haze. A _scale of Igroon_ (Hardness 20, HP 160, BT 80) recovers 4 Hit Points at the start of its wielder's turn. When you [Raise a Shield](/Actions.aspx?ID=98), you can use the [Shield Block](/Feats.aspx?ID=839) reaction with the scale of Igroon to block an attack or effect that deals acid, cold, electricity, fire, force, or sonic damage as well as physical damage. **Activate** ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Requirements** You're in an area of [bright](/Rules.aspx?ID=407) or [dim light](/Rules.aspx?ID=408); **Effect** You angle the shield to refract light. Until the start of your next turn, you gain a +4 item bonus to [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) checks to [Hide](/Actions.aspx?ID=62) and [Sneak](/Actions.aspx?ID=63) and can do so while [observed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=27). This bonus ends if you Activate another ability or use the Shield Block reaction. **Activate** ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Requirements** You're in an area of bright light; **Effect** You angle the shield at a target within 60 feet, reflecting light into its eyes. It must attempt a DC 42 Fortitude save. **Critical Success** The target is unaffected.
**Success** The target is [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=1) until its next turn begins.
**Failure** The target is blinded for 1 minute.
**Critical Failure** The target is blinded for 2d4 hours. **Activate** ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Trigger** You use Shield Block and prevent yourself from taking energy damage from a line, ray, or a direct attack, including a [_force barrage_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1536) spell; **Effect** You reflect the energy along a trajectory you choose. The effect travels only up to its remaining range, using its original parameters if it strikes other targets. **Destruction** If a deity, kaiju, spawn of a deity, titan, or being of similar power stomps on a _scale of Igroon_ while in absolute darkness, the shield is destroyed permanently.
name: Scale of Igroon

item_bonus_note: Occultism check to Decipher Writing, Identify Magic, Learn a Spell, or Recall Knowledge
markdown:
**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 180 **Usage** held in 2 hands **Bulk** 2 **Base Weapon** [Glaive](/Weapons.aspx?ID=20) --- This _+4 [major striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [greater brilliant](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1043) [keen](/Equipment.aspx?ID=300) [glaive](/Weapons.aspx?ID=20)_ binds the souls of powerful creatures it slays. The soul can't be returned to life by any means while imprisoned within the glaive and can be freed only by a deed of great benevolence or selflessness. While using the glaive as a weapon, whenever you reduce a sapient creature of 18th level or higher to 0 Hit Points or roll a critical success with a Strike against such a target, the creature must attempt a DC 50 Fortitude save. **Critical Success** No additional effect.
**Success** The glaive siphons the creature's essence, rendering it [drained 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10). If the creature dies from the Strike or while drained in this way, the creature's soul is bound in the glaive.
**Failure** As success, but the creature is drained 2.
**Critical Failure** As success, but the creature is drained 4. The glaive also has the following activations. **Activate** ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Trigger** You Strike with the _Whisperer of Souls_; **Effect** You choose whether the Strike deals lethal or [nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=188) damage. If the glaive has a reason for doing so, such as slaying a creature whose soul the glaive can absorb, it can defy your choice if you fail a DC 50 Will save. **Activate** ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Trigger** You attempt an [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) check to [Decipher Writing](/Actions.aspx?ID=22), [Identify Magic](/Actions.aspx?ID=24), [Learn a Spell](/Actions.aspx?ID=25), or [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=26); **Effect** You listen to eldritch secrets the weapon whispers, gaining a +4 item bonus to the triggering check. **Activate** ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per week; **Effect** Attempt an Occultism check as if you cast the [legend lore](/Rituals.aspx?ID=15) ritual about a subject. If you roll a success or critical success, you can repeat what the _Whisperer of Souls_ relates to you about the subject. On a critical failure, you and the glaive are drawn into a murmuring void of cold, where your mind is assaulted by strange visions for an entire week. At the end of this time, you reappear and must attempt a DC 50 Will save. **Success** When you return, you can retrain one of your skills into a [Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) skill about evil creatures or places, as if you had spent 1 week retraining.
**Failure** As success, but you are [stupefied 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=37) for 1 week.
**Critical Failure** As success, but you become [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521). If you were [holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517), you instead lose that trait. You must retrain one of your skills into a Lore about unholy creatures or places. You become obsessed with increasing your forbidden knowledge, turning to wicked forces that offer such secrets and compromising your morals further if you must. **Destruction** If the _Whisperer of Souls_ is used to slay [Shelyn's](/Deities.aspx?ID=17) [herald](/Traits.aspx?ID=330), it disintegrates, bringing about great evil in its wake.
name: Whisperer of Souls

_Note from Nethys: This item is a constituent part of the [Ursine Avenger Hood](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=200) archetype using the [archetype artifact](/Rules.aspx?ID=1961) rules._

A gift passed from warrior to warrior, this cowl is made from a [cave bear's](/Monsters.aspx?ID=48) skull and fur. Once invested, the wearer gains a +1 item bonus to [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) checks to [Command an Animal](/Actions.aspx?ID=58), which increases to +2 if the animal is a [bear](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=13). The wearer of the hood feels a constant longing for the wilderness and wild places and takes a –1 item penalty to all [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) checks to [Gather Information](/Actions.aspx?ID=49) or [Make an Impression](/Actions.aspx?ID=50) if they go more than 5 days inside a large town or city without spending at least 4 hours in the wilderness.

**Destruction** Soak the _Ursine Avenger Hood_ in the blood of a [linnorm](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=69) that is at least 16th level and then burn it in a fire that uses only oak and chrysanthemum as fuel.

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 11"
    pfs="Restricted"
>
[Corruption Cassock](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2376)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="No Alignment" url="/Rules.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Cursed" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=274" />
<trait label="Divine" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=48" />
<trait label="Focused" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=74" />
<trait label="Intelligent" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=280" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Unholy" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=521" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 190

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn garment

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Perception** +20; [precise](/Rules.aspx?ID=2406) vision 30 feet, [imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=2407) hearing 30 feet

**Communication** [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=79) ([Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Empyrean](/Languages.aspx?ID=115), four other languages)

**Skills** [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +21, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +19, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +19

**Int** +2, **Wis** +5, **Cha** +4

**Will** +20 

</column>

---        

Devotees of benevolent faiths fear and loathe corruption cassocks, each of which is dedicated to an [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521) deity. When the garment chooses a target, such as when it senses a worshipper of a deity who isn’t unholy, it alters its form to match another deity’s iconography, appearing to be a devoted vestments for that deity. Once donned, the cassock tries to subtly sway its wearer to the worship of its deity. If the wearer begins to succumb, the _corruption cassock_ gradually reverts to its true appearance, replacing its false iconography as its wearer converts.

A _corruption cassock_ functions as a [_devoted vestments_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3077) for any wearer it chooses. Once you invest the cassock, it fuses to you. While you remain loyal to another deity, you believe the cassock grants you a +2 item bonus to [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) checks and a +1 item bonus to the divine skill of the deity to whom you believe the cassock is dedicated. It does neither. The GM secretly adjusts your checks with those skills, causing you to fail at religious tasks more often. If you have multiple equal or lower item bonuses to the affected skills, the cassock takes precedence, negating those bonuses. If you convert to following the cassock’s true unholy deity, however, these bonuses function normally for you.

If you attempt to Cast a Spell that has the [holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517) trait, the cassock can force you to attempt a DC 28 Will save. On a failure, you’re [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) 1 for one minute (stupefied 2 on a critical failure). A _corruption cassock_ uses this ability to convince you that your deity has forsaken you.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You gain 1 Focus Point, which you can spend only to cast a  cleric domain spell for a domain belonging to the deity you believe the cassock is dedicated to. If you don't spend this Focus Point by the end of this turn, it's lost. However, when you use this activation, the _corruption cassock_ can instead cast a domain spell of the same rank from its deity. It does so only to confuse and dismay you, harming your faith.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Corruption Cassock

<column gap="tiny">

**Perception** +20; [precise](/Rules.aspx?ID=2406) vision 30 feet, [imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=2407) hearing 30 feet

**Communication** [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=79) ([Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Empyrean](/Languages.aspx?ID=115), four other languages)

**Skills** [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +21, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +19, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +19

**Int** +2, **Wis** +5, **Cha** +4

**Will** +20 

</column>

---        

Devotees of benevolent faiths fear and loathe corruption cassocks, each of which is dedicated to an [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521) deity. When the garment chooses a target, such as when it senses a worshipper of a deity who isn’t unholy, it alters its form to match another deity’s iconography, appearing to be a devoted vestments for that deity. Once donned, the cassock tries to subtly sway its wearer to the worship of its deity. If the wearer begins to succumb, the _corruption cassock_ gradually reverts to its true appearance, replacing its false iconography as its wearer converts.

A _corruption cassock_ functions as a [_devoted vestments_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3077) for any wearer it chooses. Once you invest the cassock, it fuses to you. While you remain loyal to another deity, you believe the cassock grants you a +2 item bonus to [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) checks and a +1 item bonus to the divine skill of the deity to whom you believe the cassock is dedicated. On a failure, you’re [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) 1 for one minute (stupefied 2 on a critical failure). A _corruption cassock_ uses this ability to convince you that your deity has forsaken you.

If you don't spend this Focus Point by the end of this turn, it's lost. However, when you use this activation, the _corruption cassock_ can instead cast a domain spell of the same rank from its deity. It does so only to confuse and dismay you, harming your faith.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

A silver charm bracelet, a _sluggish bracelet_ appears to be a [_bracelet of dashing_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=415), granting you a +1 item bonus to [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) checks. If the curse goes unrecognized, you think you can Activate it to gain a +10-foot status bonus to your Speed for 1 minute. Instead, its activation is as follows.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561))    **Effect** You take a –10-foot penalty to your Speed for 1 minute, and the bracelet fuses to you. Thereafter, it grants you no bonus to Acrobatics checks, and it imposes a –5-foot status penalty to your Speed.

</column>

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 15"
    pfs="Restricted"
>
[Unfathomable Stargazer](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2391)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Cursed" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=274" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 193

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

An _unfathomable stargazer_ is a handheld brass telescope etched with constellations, the patterns of which form bizarre, shifting runes. You can observe the night sky with an _unfathomable stargazer_, however, the constellations you glimpse through the telescope distort with expanding and contracting fields of darkness among them. You use [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) instead of Perception or [Astronomy Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) when observing the skies with this item, and gain a +2 item bonus on all checks to do so. Once you use it, it fuses to you, and you must succeed at a DC 35 Will save to use another device to observe the stars, including your naked eyes. The telescope imposes a –4 circumstance penalty to [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) checks to [Sense Direction](/Actions.aspx?ID=64) or navigate. If you critically fail this Survival check, you're subjected to [_warp mind_](/Spells.aspx?ID=369) (DC 34) as you glimpse something horrifying and alien amid the darkness.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Unfathomable Stargazer

    level="1"
    right="Item 6"
    pfs="Restricted"
>
[Majordomo Torc](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2398)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="No Alignment" url="/Rules.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Intelligent" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=280" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 195

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Perception** +13; [precise](/Rules.aspx?ID=411) vision 30 feet, [imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=412) hearing 30 feet

**Communication** speech ([Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1) and 1 imparted language)

**Skills** [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +13, [Nobility Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +13, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14) +13

**Int** +3, **Wis** +3, **Cha** +3

**Will** +14 

</column>

---        

Forged in platinum, a _majordomo torc_ is engraved with heraldic insignias along with one language's alphabet, much like a [_choker of elocution_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=422). You gain a +1 item bonus to [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14) checks and the ability to understand, speak, and write the torc's language. Your excellent elocution reduces the DC of the flat check to perform an auditory action while [deafened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=8) from 5 to 3.

 Upon being invested, the torc appoints itself your majordomo and, given the chance, takes over coordinating your social calendar, engagements, and wardrobe. A _majordomo torc_ has a prim, fussy disposition, and although it defers to you, it can grow sardonic if you frequently ignore its advice. The torc has the following activation.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** The _majordomo torc_ casts [_befitting attire_](/Spells.aspx?ID=867) on you, usually to your specifications. However the torc can also choose the appearance of the illusion for you.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Majordomo Torc

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 12"
    pfs="Restricted"
>
[Phantom Piano](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2400)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="No Alignment" url="/Rules.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Focused" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=74" />
<trait label="Intelligent" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=280" />
<trait label="Occult" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=120" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 196

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** 2 hands

**Bulk** 16
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Perception** +22; [precise](/Rules.aspx?ID=411) vision 30 feet, [imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=412) hearing 30 feet

**Communication** [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=37) (any languages the possessing spirit knew in life)

**Skills** [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +23, [Music Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +23, [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12) +25, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14) +21

**Int** +3, **Wis** +4, **Cha** +5

**Will** +22 

</column>

---        

When a skilled musician dies before reaching their full potential or accomplishing an important goal, their soul might refuse to move on to the [Boneyard](/Planes.aspx?ID=15). Rather than becoming [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=160), the spirit possesses an instrument they played in life, turning it into an intelligent item and a [virtuoso instrument](/Equipment.aspx?ID=37). (Other _phantom instruments_ exist, but most are heavy.) A _phantom piano_ offers a bargain to any bard or other skilled musician who finds it: receive musical aid as you dedicate yourself to reaching the success the spirit felt they were denied in life. The piano refuses to play for those who reject this deal. If you accept, the piano asks only that you practice with it and perform for increasingly refined and august audiences, requiring a successful [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12) check appropriate to your proficiency level. The piano sets no strict timeline, but at the GM's discretion, if you go too long without making progress, the piano can cease cooperating with you until it's satisfied with your dedication once again.

 While you keep up your end of the bargain, the piano grants you a +2 item bonus to Performance checks made with it. The piano also provides access to composition spells based on your Performance proficiency rank, as if you had the corresponding feat. The spells you can use comprise a cumulative list, but you can cast them only when using the piano. You must spend the actions and Focus Points required to cast these spells.

**Trained**  [_courageous anthem_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1763), [_uplifting overture_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1779)

**Expert**  [hymn of healing](/Feats.aspx?ID=4574), [_rallying anthem_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1773)

**Master**  [_symphony of the unfettered heart_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1777)

**Legendary**  [_soothing ballad_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1776)

 A _phantom piano_ has the following activations.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561))    **Effect** The piano shrinks down to a figurine of light Bulk compact enough to store in a pouch, or from figurine form back to instrument form. In figurine form, the piano doesn't function as an instrument or provide magic other than this activation.

**Activate** <actions string="Free Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You gain 1 Focus Point, which you can spend only to Cast a Spell the piano provides you.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Phantom Piano

markdown:
**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 204 **Usage** worn --- Blessed by [Nivi Rhombodazzle](/Deities.aspx?ID=44), this striking silver necklace is adorned with sparkling sapphires that draw the eye of any who see it. Over the years, this necklace has appeared in many paintings and illustrations, usually around the neck of a carefree and daring adventurer or a stylish rake gambling vast sums of coin. Finding this relic is always seen as a sign of great fortune to come, but when that luck runs out, the necklace is lost just as quickly. Wearing the relic, you quickly realize how distracting it can be, granting you a +1 item bonus on [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) checks made to [Feint](/Actions.aspx?ID=48) and on [Games Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) checks made to gamble or determine the outcome of a game of chance. --- **Minor Gifts**: [beginner's luck](/Relics.aspx?ID=97), [whammy](/Relics.aspx?ID=99)
**Major Gifts**: [clean luck](/Relics.aspx?ID=100), [perception filter](/Relics.aspx?ID=52)
**Grand Gift**: [against all odds](/Relics.aspx?ID=102)
name: Fortune's Favor
