markdown:
**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 205 **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** L --- A famed detective once owned the long, curved _sleuth's pipe_, taking it with them on investigations, keeping it in hand or mouth while cogitating. The pipe might have passed down through generations. Or, when an owner dies without heirs, it could go up for auction, be given to family friends, or remain at the scene of a crime to be found later. The _sleuth's pipe_ has sapience that eventually awakens, allowing the relic to act as a helpful assistant in investigations. Even while this sapience sleeps within the pipe, it grants you a +1 item bonus to Perception checks to [Sense Motive](/Actions.aspx?ID=85), and if you're an [investigator](/Classes.aspx?ID=13), any checks you make to [Pursue a Lead](/Actions.aspx?ID=544). --- **Minor Gifts**: [beginner's luck](/Relics.aspx?ID=97), [repository of knowledge](/Relics.aspx?ID=51)
**Major Gifts**: [intelligent relic](/Relics.aspx?ID=109), [steal luck](/Relics.aspx?ID=101)
**Grand Gift**: [mental bastion](/Relics.aspx?ID=54)
name: Sleuth's Pipe

item_bonus_note: If you're an Investigator, any checks made to Pursue a Lead
markdown:
**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 205 **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** L --- A famed detective once owned the long, curved _sleuth's pipe_, taking it with them on investigations, keeping it in hand or mouth while cogitating. The pipe might have passed down through generations. Or, when an owner dies without heirs, it could go up for auction, be given to family friends, or remain at the scene of a crime to be found later. The _sleuth's pipe_ has sapience that eventually awakens, allowing the relic to act as a helpful assistant in investigations. Even while this sapience sleeps within the pipe, it grants you a +1 item bonus to Perception checks to [Sense Motive](/Actions.aspx?ID=85), and if you're an [investigator](/Classes.aspx?ID=13), any checks you make to [Pursue a Lead](/Actions.aspx?ID=544). --- **Minor Gifts**: [beginner's luck](/Relics.aspx?ID=97), [repository of knowledge](/Relics.aspx?ID=51)
**Major Gifts**: [intelligent relic](/Relics.aspx?ID=109), [steal luck](/Relics.aspx?ID=101)
**Grand Gift**: [mental bastion](/Relics.aspx?ID=54)
name: Sleuth's Pipe

item_bonus_note: Recall Knowledge about historical subjects
markdown:
**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 205 **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** L --- The origins of the _watch of lost ages_, a silver pocket watch, are enigmatic. Its construction matches no known culture, and its appearance shifts back and forth from battered and tarnished to shiny and new without cause or apparent reason. The watch exudes a sense of melancholy and loss, and anyone holding it is acutely aware of the impermanence of all things and the march of time, moving ever forward. This relic might show up in a mysterious ruin or spontaneously appear in a spellcaster's hand after they cast a time spell. The relic grants you a +1 item bonus to [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=26) about historical subjects. --- **Minor Gifts**: [fervor](/Relics.aspx?ID=93), [sands of the hourglass](/Relics.aspx?ID=104)
**Major Gifts**: [fervent spell](/Relics.aspx?ID=95), [temporal pulse](/Relics.aspx?ID=106)
**Grand Gift**: [time reversal](/Relics.aspx?ID=108)
name: Watch of Lost Ages

item_bonus_note: Checks to identify celestial bodies
markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 2+"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Astrolabe](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2411)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Gatewalkers</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #188: They Watched the Stars](/Sources.aspx?ID=194) pg. 79

<row gap="medium">

**Hands** 2

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="" /><row>1 minute (Interact)</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Astrolabes can be used for navigation in unfamiliar or featureless locations. To use an astrolabe, the holder must be trained in [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16). By spending 1 minute to measure the height of the stars and planets, a holder who knows the time and date can determine the latitude, and a holder who knows their latitude can determine the date and time. An astrolabe also grants a +1 item bonus to checks to identify celestial bodies. A standard astrolabe functions only on steady ground.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 1"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Eyecatcher](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2452)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Adjustment" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=380" />

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Firebrands](/Sources.aspx?ID=196) pg. 82

**Price** 2 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** applied to a weapon

</row>

**Access** Second-mark members of the Firebrands have access to this item.

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

An eyecatcher is a simple tassel, reflective basket hilt, or other accessory attached to a weapon to serve as a distraction. You gain a +1 item bonus to [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) checks to [Feint](/Actions.aspx?ID=48) while using a weapon with an eyecatcher attached. An eyecatcher affects the balancing of a weapon, making it slightly more unwieldy. This increases the weapon's Bulk by 1 and grants a –1 penalty to damage rolls with the weapon.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Eyecatcher

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 1"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Subtle Armor](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2453)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Adjustment" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=380" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Firebrands](/Sources.aspx?ID=196) pg. 83

**Price** 5 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** applied to armor

</row>

**Access** Second-mark members of the Firebrands have access to this item.

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Using fabrics and fashion accessories, you disguise your armor to look like everyday clothing. After applying this adjustment, you can attempt a [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) check to [Conceal an Object](/Actions.aspx?ID=61) to hide the nature of your armor. You gain a +1 item bonus to this check. The added weight and care make it more difficult to move around in the armor, increasing the armor's check penalty by 1, its Strength entry value by 2, and its Bulk by 1.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Subtle Armor

item_bonus_note: Acrobatics checks to Balance
markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 5+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Boots of Free Running](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2456)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Transmutation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=157" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Firebrands](/Sources.aspx?ID=196) pg. 84

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn shoes

**Bulk** 1
</row>

**Access** Second-mark members of the Firebrands have access to this item.

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

These comfortable and practical boots slip on easily and fill you with boundless energy. The treads of these boots provide exceptional traction, with improved grip on surfaces you would traditionally have difficulty traversing. While wearing the boots, you gain a +1 item bonus to [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) checks to [Balance](/Actions.aspx?ID=28) and to [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) checks to [High Jump](/Actions.aspx?ID=36) and [Long Jump](/Actions.aspx?ID=37).

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> envision;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** The traction of your boots improves, allowing you to run along vertical surfaces for 1 minute. When you Stride, you can run up solid vertical surfaces, like walls, at your full Speed. You must start your movement on a horizontal surface. If you end a Stride off the ground, you must Stride along the surface again until you reach a horizontal surface or you begin to fall (though you can [Grab an Edge](/Actions.aspx?ID=95), if applicable). If you have means of walking on water, such as with [_water walk_](/Spells.aspx?ID=371) or similar abilities, you can also run along flimsy vertical surfaces, as well as vertical liquids such as a waterfall.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

The bonus is +2. You can activate the _moderate boots of free running_ once per hour instead of once per day.

level="2"
right="Item 17"
pfs="Standard"
>
Boots of Free Running (Greater)

**Bulk** 1

</row>

---

The bonus is +3. You can activate the _greater boots of free running_ without a frequency limit.

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 5+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Boots of Free Running](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2456)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Transmutation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=157" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Firebrands](/Sources.aspx?ID=196) pg. 84

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn shoes

**Bulk** 1
</row>

**Access** Second-mark members of the Firebrands have access to this item.

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

These comfortable and practical boots slip on easily and fill you with boundless energy. The treads of these boots provide exceptional traction, with improved grip on surfaces you would traditionally have difficulty traversing. While wearing the boots, you gain a +1 item bonus to [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) checks to [Balance](/Actions.aspx?ID=28) and to [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) checks to [High Jump](/Actions.aspx?ID=36) and [Long Jump](/Actions.aspx?ID=37).

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> envision;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** The traction of your boots improves, allowing you to run along vertical surfaces for 1 minute. When you Stride, you can run up solid vertical surfaces, like walls, at your full Speed. You must start your movement on a horizontal surface. If you end a Stride off the ground, you must Stride along the surface again until you reach a horizontal surface or you begin to fall (though you can [Grab an Edge](/Actions.aspx?ID=95), if applicable). If you have means of walking on water, such as with [_water walk_](/Spells.aspx?ID=371) or similar abilities, you can also run along flimsy vertical surfaces, as well as vertical liquids such as a waterfall.

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 4+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Diver's Gloves](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2461)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Abjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=2" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Firebrands](/Sources.aspx?ID=196) pg. 85

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn gloves

**Bulk** L
</row>

**Access** Second-mark members of the Firebrands have access to this item.

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

These black leather gloves fit snuggly, running up the length of your arm to your elbow. Each glove has an angular fin running along the outside edge of your arms, which forms a narrow triangle when your hands meet in a diving position. While wearing the gloves, you gain a +1 item bonus to [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) checks to [Swim](/Actions.aspx?ID=39).

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> envision;   **Trigger** You would take [damage from falling](/Rules.aspx?ID=402) into water;  **Effect** The gloves create a hydrodynamic barrier around you, dispersing the damage that would be done to you during a high dive. You take damage as if the fall were half the distance, to a minimum fall of 5 feet. You then Swim with the effects of a critical success for the check.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 4"
pfs="Standard"
>
Diver's Gloves (Lesser)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Firebrands](/Sources.aspx?ID=196) pg. 85

**Price** 85 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 9"
pfs="Standard"
>
Diver's Gloves (Moderate)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Firebrands](/Sources.aspx?ID=196) pg. 85

**Price** 650 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

The item bonus is +2. The gloves grant you a swim Speed equal to half of your land Speed.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 15"
pfs="Standard"
>
Diver's Gloves (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Firebrands](/Sources.aspx?ID=196) pg. 85

**Price** 6,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

The item bonus is +3. The gloves grant you a swim Speed equal to your land Speed or 30 feet, whichever is higher.
name: Diver's Gloves (Lesser)

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 2+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Stone of Encouragement](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2467)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Divination" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=47" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Firebrands](/Sources.aspx?ID=196) pg. 87

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

**Access** Second-mark members of the Firebrands have access to this item.

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This smooth, round, gray stone fits neatly into the palm of your hand and feels comfortable to hold regardless of the size or shape of your hand. While you're holding the stone, it telepathically speaks to you at regular intervals in generic words of encouragement, such as “You can do it!” and “Let's go!” The encouraging words manifest in the voice of your internal monologue or take on the characteristics of the voice of someone you trust. Despite the similarity, you're aware that the stone is telepathically projecting its voice and can differentiate between the stone's voice and your internal monologue or the voice of the person you trust.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> command;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You ask the stone for help with a task. It speaks a few motivating words related to the task at hand, which grants you a +1 item bonus to the first skill check you attempt within 1 minute. You become temporarily immune to the effects of all _stones of encouragement_, even ones other than the one you activated, for 1 day. You can still hear the encouraging words of different stones if you hold them, but they don't provide any benefits.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 2"
pfs="Standard"
>
Stone of Encouragement
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Firebrands](/Sources.aspx?ID=196) pg. 87

**Price** 30 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 8"
pfs="Standard"
>
Stone of Encouragement (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Firebrands](/Sources.aspx?ID=196) pg. 87

**Price** 475 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

The item bonus is +2.

    right="Item 20"
    pfs=""
>
[The Big Sky](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2475)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Stolen Fate</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Unique" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=161" />
<trait label="Abjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=2" />
<trait label="Artifact" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=253" />
<trait label="Harrow Court" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=496" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #190: The Choosing](/Sources.aspx?ID=198) pg. 75

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** carried

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

_The Big Sky_ represents widespread change or liberation. As long as you have _The Big Sky_ invested, you gain a +2 item bonus to Perception checks made while you are flying outside during the day and to any check made to [Escape](/Actions.aspx?ID=79). The bonus increases to +3 if you are at least 17th level.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> envision;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** For 10 minutes, you and up to four creatures you touch ignore [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=453) and effects that would give a circumstance penalty to Speed. If you are at least 17th level, the targets also ignore greater difficult terrain.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

item_bonus_note: Society checks to Recall Knowledge about someone no longer alive (+3 at 17th level)
markdown:
May contain spoilers from Stolen Fate **Source** [Pathfinder #190: The Choosing](/Sources.aspx?ID=198) pg. 77, [Stolen Fate Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=197) pg. 5 **Usage** carried --- _The Empty Throne_ represents great loss and wisdom from those who are now gone. As long as you have _The Empty Throne_ invested, it grants you a +2 item bonus to [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14) and [Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) checks made to [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=26) regarding individuals who are no longer alive (such as checks to learn about Taldor's historical founder, but not to learn about its current empress). This increases to a +3 item bonus if you are at least 17th level. **Activate** envision; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You overwhelm a target you can see within 60 feet with feelings of helplessness and loss. The creature must attempt a Will DC equal to your class DC. **Critical Success** The target is unaffected.
**Success** The target can't benefit from circumstance or status bonuses for 1 round.
**Failure** The target can't benefit from circumstance or status bonuses for 1 day, and the target's allies are unable to benefit from circumstance or status bonuses while within 15 feet of the target.
**Critical Failure** As failure, except the duration is 1 week.
name: The Empty Throne

>
[The Fiend](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2479)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Stolen Fate</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Unique" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=161" />
<trait label="Artifact" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=253" />
<trait label="Harrow Court" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=496" />
<trait label="Illusion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=92" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #190: The Choosing](/Sources.aspx?ID=198) pg. 77

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** carried

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

_The Fiend_ represents the inevitability of great calamities and pervasive dangers. As long as you have _The Fiend_ invested, when you repeat a non-Strike action you previously took in the same round, you gain a +2 item bonus to checks to perform that action. This increases to a +3 item bonus if you are at least 17th level.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> envision, Interact;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You wave the card to create a vision of destruction around you; you cast [_phantasmal calamity_](/Spells.aspx?ID=218), except the area is a 20-foot emanation. The level of the spell is one-half your level, rounded down (minimum 6th level), and the save DC is your class DC. You are immune to the effects of your own _phantasmal calamity_.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: The Fiend

---

_The Liar_ represents obsession or treacherous love. As long as you have _The Liar_ invested, it grants you a +2 item bonus to [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) checks to [Lie](/Actions.aspx?ID=47). This increases to a +3 item bonus if you're at least 17th level. If the implausibility of your lies prompts a circumstance penalty to your Deception checks or an increase to your Deception DC, reduce that penalty or increase by half.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> envision, Interact;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You touch a weapon and instill faithlessness within it. The weapon gains the [backbiting curse](/Curses.aspx?ID=3). The curse lasts until you use this activation again. If you're at least 17th level, you can instead use this activation on a weapon within 30 feet. If the weapon is carried by a creature, it can resist the effect with a successful Will save against your class DC.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> envision, Interact;  **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** You would be afflicted by a [curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=38) or a [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46);  **Effect** You mimic the turning of a key in a lock and free yourself from your unpleasant fate. You attempt to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) the triggering affliction, using half your level (rounded up) as the counteract level and a counteract check modifier equal to your class DC – 10.

item_bonus_note: Arcana checks to Recall Knowledge (+3 at 17th level)
markdown:
May contain spoilers from Stolen Fate **Source** [Pathfinder #190: The Choosing](/Sources.aspx?ID=198) pg. 79 **Usage** carried --- _The Silent Hag_ represents insight, treacherous secrets, and strife. As long as you have _The Silent Hag_ invested, it grants you a +2 item bonus to [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10), [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11), or [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) checks to [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=26)—when you invest the card, choose which of these four skills gains the bonus. You can change the skill by investing the card again. This increases to a +3 item bonus if you are at least 17th level. **Activate** envision; **Frequency** once per hour; **Effect** You choose to lose one of your senses and gain another until you use this activation again. Choose from one of the following:
  • Your eyes are replaced by dark pits that see only death. You become blinded and gain [lifesense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=21), an [imprecise sense](/Rules.aspx?ID=412) out to 60 feet. Lifesense allows you to see the vital essence of living and [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=160) creatures, distinguishing between the positive energy animating living creatures and negative energy animating undead creatures, much as sight distinguishes colors.
  • Your ears close up then become covered with hairs that are sensitive to movement. You become [deafened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=8), but you can't be flanked.
  • Your mouth seals over with flesh. You lose the ability to speak, but you do not need to [breathe](/Rules.aspx?ID=468).
name: The Silent Hag

As long as you have _The Silent Hag_ invested, it grants you a +2 item bonus to [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10), [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11), or [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) checks to [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=26)—when you invest the card, choose which of these four skills gains the bonus. You can change the skill by investing the card again. This increases to a +3 item bonus if you are at least 17th level. **Activate** envision; **Frequency** once per hour; **Effect** You choose to lose one of your senses and gain another until you use this activation again. Choose from one of the following:
  • Your eyes are replaced by dark pits that see only death. You become blinded and gain [lifesense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=21), an [imprecise sense](/Rules.aspx?ID=412) out to 60 feet. Lifesense allows you to see the vital essence of living and [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=160) creatures, distinguishing between the positive energy animating living creatures and negative energy animating undead creatures, much as sight distinguishes colors.
  • Your ears close up then become covered with hairs that are sensitive to movement. You become [deafened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=8), but you can't be flanked.
  • Your mouth seals over with flesh. You lose the ability to speak, but you do not need to [breathe](/Rules.aspx?ID=468).
name: The Silent Hag

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 20"
    pfs=""
>
[The Rakshasa](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2488)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Stolen Fate</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Unique" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=161" />
<trait label="Artifact" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=253" />
<trait label="Harrow Court" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=496" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Necromancy" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=117" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #190: The Choosing](/Sources.aspx?ID=198) pg. 81

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** carried

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

_The Rakshasa_ represents domination of others to support your own schemes. When you invest _The Rakshasa_, identify a single willing creature within 30 feet as your pledged follower. If you're at least 15th level, you can have up to two pledged followers at once. At 18th level, you can have up to three pledged followers. If you have the maximum pledged followers active and select a new one, a previous pledged follower of your choice is no longer your pledged follower. You gain an item bonus equal to your current number of pledged followers to all [Coerce](/Actions.aspx?ID=52) attempts.

**Activate** <actions string="Free Action" />  ;  **Frequency** once per minute;   **Effect** You regain Hit Points equal to twice your level, and a pledged follower of your choice loses Hit Points equal to your level (no effects apply that would decrease this Hit Point loss). If this loss kills your pledged follower, you also gain temporary Hit Points equal to your level. The pledged follower is temporarily immune to this activation for 24 hours.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: The Rakshasa

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 20"
    pfs=""
>
[The Snakebite](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2489)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Stolen Fate</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Unique" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=161" />
<trait label="Artifact" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=253" />
<trait label="Harrow Court" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=496" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Necromancy" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=117" />
<trait label="Poison" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=126" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #190: The Choosing](/Sources.aspx?ID=198) pg. 81

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** carried

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

_The Snakebite_ represents poison, assassination, and disharmony. As long as you have _The Snakebite_ invested, it grants you a +2 item bonus to [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) and [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) checks you make for initiative. This increases to a +3 item bonus if you're at least 17th level. The DCs of your [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126) effects have a +1 item bonus to targets that are [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) to you when you afflict the target with the poison (the DC retains this bonus for the target's subsequent saves against the affliction, even if the target is no longer flat-footed to you).

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> envision, Interact;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You cast [_purple worm sting_](/Spells.aspx?ID=242) on a creature within your reach, with a DC equal to your class DC.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: The Snakebite

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 20"
    pfs=""
>
[The Trumpet](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2491)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Stolen Fate</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Unique" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=161" />
<trait label="Artifact" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=253" />
<trait label="Evocation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=65" />
<trait label="Harrow Court" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=496" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #190: The Choosing](/Sources.aspx?ID=198) pg. 81

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** carried

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

_The Trumpet_ represents timely warning. As long as you have _The Trumpet_ invested, you (and only you) hear the sound of trumpets when you're about to be attacked. This grants you a +2 item bonus to initiative checks made with hearing-based Perception checks. This bonus increases to +3 if you're at least 17th level.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> envision;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** A blast of inspiring noise erupts from the card in a 60-foot cone. Creatures in the area take 14d6 sonic damage (with a [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Fortitude save equal to your class DC). Creatures that fail the save are [deafened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=8) for 1 round (1 minute on a critical failure). The damage increases to 16d6 if you're at least 13th level, to 18d6 if you're at least 15th level, to 20d6 if you're at least 17th level, and to 22d6 if you're at least 19th level.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: The Trumpet

>
[The Vision](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2492)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Stolen Fate</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Unique" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=161" />
<trait label="Artifact" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=253" />
<trait label="Divination" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=47" />
<trait label="Harrow Court" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=496" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #190: The Choosing](/Sources.aspx?ID=198) pg. 81

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** carried

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

_The Vision_ represents arcane knowledge. As long as you have _The Vision_ invested, it grants you a +2 item bonus to all checks made to [Identify Magic](/Actions.aspx?ID=24). This increases to a +3 item bonus if you are at least 17th level.

**Activate** <actions string="Free Action" /> envision;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** A visible bolt of blue energy bursts from your head. Attempt to [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=26) about a subject, rolling the check with the most appropriate [Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) skill check. This check always resolves as if you were master in the most appropriate Lore skill to Recall Knowledge on the subject (or if you're at least 15th level, as if you were legendary in that Lore skill). If you're already master (or legendary) in that Lore, the result of your Recall Knowledge check is one degree of success better than it would otherwise be.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: The Vision

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 20"
    pfs=""
>
[The Cyclone](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2503)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Stolen Fate</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Unique" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=161" />
<trait label="Artifact" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=253" />
<trait label="Evocation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=65" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #191: The Destiny War](/Sources.aspx?ID=199) pg. 73

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** carried

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

_The Cyclone_ represents tumultuous evil plots. As long as it is invested, _The Cyclone_ bolsters you with helpful winds that grant you a +2 item bonus to [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) checks made to [Climb](/Actions.aspx?ID=33), [High Jump](/Actions.aspx?ID=36), [Long Jump](/Actions.aspx?ID=37), and [Disarm](/Actions.aspx?ID=41). This increases to a +3 item bonus if you are at least 17th level.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> envision, Interact;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You cast [_whirlwind_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1033). You do not treat the squares in the _whirlwind_ as difficult terrain, but all other creatures do. The level of the spell is one-half your level, rounded down (minimum 8th level), and the save DC is your class DC.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

_The Hidden Truth_ represents the act of observing something beyond the obvious to gain obscure lore. As long as this card is invested, you gain a +2 item bonus to [Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) checks made to [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=26), and to all checks made to [Research](/Actions.aspx?ID=519) while using the [Research](/Rules.aspx?ID=1205) subsystem from the _Gamemastery Guide_. This increases to a +3 bonus if you are at least 17th level.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> envision, Interact;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You cast [_true seeing_](/Spells.aspx?ID=344). The level of the spell is one-half your level, rounded down (minimum 6th level). If you're at least 17th-level, you can affect up to three adjacent willing targets with this spell at the same time as you cast it.

</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Stolen Fate</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Unique" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=161" />
<trait label="Artifact" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=253" />
<trait label="Divination" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=47" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #191: The Destiny War](/Sources.aspx?ID=199) pg. 76

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** carried

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

_The Marriage_ symbolizes a union of body, mind, spirit, or any of the three. When _The Marriage_ is invested, you gain a +2 item bonus to attempts made to [Request](/Actions.aspx?ID=51). This increases to a +3 item bonus at 17th level.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> envision, Interact;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** Choose one adjacent willing creature. By brushing _The Marriage_ against their body, you form a magical bond with that creature that persists until you activate _The Marriage_ again to form a bond with a different creature. While the bond persists, you and the other creature can communicate via [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=37) to a distance of 120 feet. As long as you and the target are on the same plane of existence and are both alive, you each remain aware of the other's state—you know the other's direction from you, distance from you, and any conditions affecting them. If your bonded target becomes [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=1), [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=5), [controlled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=6), [fascinated](/Conditions.aspx?ID=14), [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19), [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=35), or [stunned](/Conditions.aspx?ID=36), you can use a reaction to attempt to [counter](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) the condition affecting the target, which also ends the bond between you and the target. The modifier on this counter check is equal to your class DC – 10. If you fail to counter the condition, that condition also afflicts you, and the bond with the other still ends.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

_The Midwife_ represents the arrival of new life or new information, particularly via the aid of another. As long as you have _The Midwife_ invested, it grants a +2 item bonus to [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=9) checks. This increases to a +3 bonus if you are at least 17th level.

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> command;  **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** A creature you can sense within 60 feet would die or be destroyed;  **Effect** You prevent the target from dying or being destroyed and restore to the target 6d8+24 Hit Points. This effect can prevent a [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effect or [_disintegrate_](/Spells.aspx?ID=76) from slaying a target. The amount of healing granted increases to 7d8+28 if you're at least 15th level, 8d8+32 if you're at least 17th level, and 9d8+36 if you're at least 19th level.

This pair of silver bangles is engraved with images of crowns. While wearing the bangles, you are filled with an overwhelming sense of assertiveness and a more commanding presence. You gain a +3 item bonus to [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) checks. When you invest the bangles, you either increase your Charisma score by 2 or increase it to 18, whichever is higher.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> command, envision;  **Frequency** once per hour;   **Effect** You cast a 3rd-level [_fear_](/Spells.aspx?ID=110) spell (DC 38).

You gain a +1 circumstance bonus to checks against the triggering creature for 1 round. You also gain 15 temporary Hit Points. You lose any remaining temporary Hit Points after 1 minute.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 17"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Bracers of Hammers](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2526)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Stolen Fate</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Apex" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=10" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Transmutation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=157" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #192: Worst of All Possible Worlds](/Sources.aspx?ID=203) pg. 69

**Price** 15,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn bracers

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

These brilliant golden bracers feature a hammer motif as part of their engravings. While wearing them, your body surges with strength and you gain a +3 item bonus to [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) checks and a +1 circumstance bonus to Athletics checks to [Disarm](/Actions.aspx?ID=41) and [Trip](/Actions.aspx?ID=40). If you succeed, but not critically succeed, at your check to Disarm a creature, the effects of your check last until the start of your next turn, instead until the start of the target's turn. If you succeed at a check to Trip a creature, it takes 1d6 bludgeoning damage or 4d6 bludgeoning damage on a critical success. When you invest the bracers, you either increase your Strength score by 2 or increase it to 18, whichever would give you a higher score.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> Interact;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You reach for a foe and attempt to topple them with a powerful flip. Attempt an Athletics check to Trip a creature within reach. On a success, the creature is knocked [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=31) and takes 10d6 bludgeoning damage (double on a critical hit). If you knock the creature prone, it must also attempt a DC 35 Reflex save. On a failure, it drops any items it's holding.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Bracers of Hammers

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 17"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Coronet of Stars](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2527)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Stolen Fate</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Apex" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=10" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Necromancy" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=117" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #192: Worst of All Possible Worlds](/Sources.aspx?ID=203) pg. 69

**Price** 15,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn headwear

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This short golden crown bears a number of gems, each placed in the center of an engraving of a star. While wearing the crown, you are overcome with a nurturing instinct and understand the best ways to help others. You gain a +3 item bonus to [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=9) checks.

 When you invest the crown, you either increase your Wisdom score by 2 or increase it to 18, whichever would give you a higher score.

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> envision;  **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** A living creature within 60 feet would die;  **Effect** You reach out to the creature with a lifeline. The triggering creature receives the effects of [_breath of life_](/Spells.aspx?ID=29), except they recover 7d8 Hit Points.

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> command;  **Frequency** once per hour; **Trigger** An ally within 60 feet fails a non-[secret](/Traits.aspx?ID=142) check;  **Effect** You call out to your ally and offer a reassurance. The triggering ally rerolls the failed check and takes the higher result. This is a [fortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=76) effect.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Coronet of Stars

    level="1"
    right="Item 17"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Cowl of Keys](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2528)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Stolen Fate</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Apex" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=10" />
<trait label="Conjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=33" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #192: Worst of All Possible Worlds](/Sources.aspx?ID=203) pg. 70

**Price** 15,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn cloak

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This simple, tattered cloak is a faded green and features small, stylized keys sewn throughout its length. While wearing the cloak, you fade into the shadows and gain a +3 item bonus to [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) checks. When you invest the cloak, you either increase your Dexterity score by 2 or increase it to 18, whichever would give you a higher score.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> Interact;     **Effect** You brush your hand against the cloak and your fingers transform into picks, tension wrenches, skeleton keys, and any other tools, providing you with a full set of [thieves' tools](/Equipment.aspx?ID=58). This transformation doesn't otherwise hamper the normal use of your fingers or hands. These tools never break when you critically fail your check to [Pick a Lock](/Actions.aspx?ID=70). You can Activate the cloak again to revert your fingers to normal.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> envision, Interact;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You place your hand against a wall, floor, or ceiling. You create a magical doorway within the surface that a creature can move through, as if the door had always been there. The doorway can penetrate up to 10 feet of material; particularly thick material, such as heavy stone walls, can cause this effect to fail, expending its use for the day. You can use an Interact action on the door to remove it from the surface and return it to its previous shape.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Cowl of Keys

>
[Sash of Books](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2529)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Stolen Fate</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Apex" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=10" />
<trait label="Divination" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=47" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #192: Worst of All Possible Worlds](/Sources.aspx?ID=203) pg. 70

**Price** 15,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn garment

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This elegant black sash features stylized crimson books embroidered at either end and a set of simple tassels. While wearing the sash, you feel as if you have a better understanding of the people and life around you. You gain a +3 item bonus to [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14) checks.

 When you invest the sash, you either increase your Intelligence score by 2 or increase it to 18, whichever would give you a higher score.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> envision;  **Frequency** once per minute;   **Effect** You focus on a creature you can see and attempt to gain an instinctual understanding of the creature. You attempt a check to [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=26) about the creature, rolling twice and taking the higher result. If you roll a critical failure on this check, you get a failure instead. Regardless of the result, you gain a +1 circumstance bonus to attack rolls against the creature and to your AC and saving throws against attacks and effects from the creature for 1 minute. This bonus increases to +2 if you critically succeeded your check to Recall Knowledge.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> command, envision;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You become keenly aware of your surroundings and become able to anticipate dangers. You gain the effects of [_foresight_](/Spells.aspx?ID=127).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Sash of Books

    level="1"
    right="Item 20"
    pfs=""
>
[The Betrayal](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2533)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Stolen Fate</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Unique" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=161" />
<trait label="Artifact" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=253" />
<trait label="Enchantment" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=61" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #192: Worst of All Possible Worlds](/Sources.aspx?ID=203) pg. 72

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** carried

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

_The Betrayal_ represents selfishness and envy, twisting outcomes toward unfavorable or even devastating results. As long as _The Betrayal_ is invested, you become more adept at hiding who you really are and tricking targets into positions of vulnerability. You gain a +2 item bonus on [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) checks made to [Impersonate](/Actions.aspx?ID=46) or [Feint](/Actions.aspx?ID=48). This increases to a +3 bonus if you are at least 17th level.

**Activate** <actions string="Free Action" /> envision;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You turn the tables on your foes, bringing one of them into your ranks and compelling them to attack their allies. You cast [_dominate_](/Spells.aspx?ID=87) on a creature, but can only issue commands to attack or otherwise harm, betray, or inconvenience its allies. The level of the spell is one-half your level, rounded down (minimum 6th level), and the save DC is your class DC.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: The Betrayal

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 20"
    pfs=""
>
[The Courtesan](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2534)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Stolen Fate</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Unique" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=161" />
<trait label="Artifact" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=253" />
<trait label="Illusion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=92" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #192: Worst of All Possible Worlds](/Sources.aspx?ID=203) pg. 72

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** carried

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

_The Courtesan_ represents political intrigue and social niceties. As long as _The Courtesan_ is invested, you gain a +2 item bonus to [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) checks to [Make an Impression](/Actions.aspx?ID=50). This increases to a +3 item bonus if you are at least 17th level.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> envision, Interact;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You pass the card over your face and become exactly who you need to be; you cast [_illusory disguise_](/Spells.aspx?ID=159). The level of the spell is one-half your level, rounded down (minimum 6th level). You can change your appearance again at any time during the duration by using a three-action activity, which has the [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32) trait. While under these effects, your [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) checks to [Impersonate](/Actions.aspx?ID=46) are one degree of success greater when you use them against a creature that is a lower level than yourself.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: The Courtesan

markdown:
May contain spoilers from Stolen Fate **Source** [Pathfinder #192: Worst of All Possible Worlds](/Sources.aspx?ID=203) pg. 73 **Usage** carried --- _The Crows_ represent taking things through violence or force, particularly when done through agility and swiftness rather than brawn. As long as _The Crows_ is invested, you can attempt to [Grapple](/Actions.aspx?ID=35) targets with [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) checks, and if you're at least trained in [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=17), to [Disarm](/Actions.aspx?ID=41) with Thievery checks. When you do so, you gain a +2 item bonus on the attempt, or a +3 item bonus if you're at least 17th level. **Activate** envision, Interact ([teleportation](/Traits.aspx?ID=156)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You hold the card in your hand, and with a flick of the wrist it vanishes, only to be replaced by an object of your choice within 30 feet. The object must be 2 Bulk or less. If that object is unattended, it teleports into your hand automatically. If the object is attended by a creature, you must make a Thievery check against the creature's Reflex DC. **Critical Success** The object teleports into your hand.
**Success** The object vanishes from the target's possession and lands on the ground at a point at a midway point between you two.
**Failure** The object does not vanish, but the creature's grip on it falters and if it doesn't spend an Interact action on its next turn to grip the item, it drops to the ground.
**Critical Failure** You fail to manipulate the object entirely.
name: The Crows

_The Dance_ represents the delicately balanced rhythms of the universe and the ability stay in step with existence. As long as _The Dance_ is invested, you gain a +2 item bonus on [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12) checks. This increases to a +3 item bonus if you are at least 17th level.

**Activate** <actions string="Free Action" /> envision;  **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** You are about to roll initiative;  **Effect** Your body begins to move with anticipation, creating a trail of afterimages for a moment. You can roll initiative with a Performance check. Regardless of what skill you use to roll for initiative, roll the check three times and take the result of your choice. This is a [fortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=76) effect.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<traits>

_The Eclipse_ represents self-doubt and loss of purpose, as well as a loss of faith. As long as _The Eclipse_ is invested, you gain a +2 item bonus on [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) checks. This increases to a +3 bonus if you are at least 17th level.

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> envision ([mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106)) **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** A foe that you're aware of and who is within 60 feet achieves a critical success on a saving throw or a Strike;  **Effect** You produce an aura of doubt that overwhelms all foes in a 30-foot emanation and reduces [bright light](/Rules.aspx?ID=407) in that area to [dim light](/Rules.aspx?ID=408), as if the region were under an eclipse. The triggering creature's critical success is reduced to a regular success. All further saving throws or Strikes attempted by enemies within the emanation suffer a –2 item penalty; saving throws or Strikes attempted by the triggering enemy suffer a –3 penalty instead. You can sustain this aura of doubt for up to 1 minute.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

_The Inquisitor_ represents the search for the truth and the power to see through lies. As long as you have _The Inquisitor_ invested, the results of your Perception Checks to [Sense Motive](/Actions.aspx?ID=85) against targets that are equal to or lower than your level are increased by one degree of success. Against targets that are higher level than you, you instead gain a +2 item bonus to your check. This bonus increases to +3 if you are at least 17th level.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> envision, Interact ([linguistic](/Traits.aspx?ID=101), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You swipe the card over the head of an adjacent creature to delve into their thoughts. You cast [_mind probe_](/Spells.aspx?ID=193), but as a two-action spell rather than one that takes 1 minute to cast. The level of the spell is one-half your level, rounded down (minimum 5th level), and the save DC is your class DC.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

_The Trader_ represents trades and exchanges of information. As long as you have _The Trader_ invested, you gain a +2 item bonus to [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) checks made to [Gather Information](/Actions.aspx?ID=49). This increases to a +3 item bonus if you are at least 17th level.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> envision, Interact;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** _The Trader_ allows you to make an exchange with yourself. Press the card to your brow, and then select two skills. Your proficiency rank with those two skills swaps for 1 hour. For example, if you were a master in [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) and untrained in [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4), you would become untrained in Athletics and a master in Crafting. If making this swap would break prerequisites to feats, you lose access to those feats until your skill proficiencies change back in an hour.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<trait label="Unique" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=161" />

</traits>

**Usage** carried

---

_The Unicorn_ represents finding what one seeks. As long as you have _The Unicorn_ invested, you gain a +2 item bonus on Perception checks to [Seek](/Actions.aspx?ID=84). This increases to a +3 item bonus if you're at least 17th level.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> envision, Interact;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You pass _The Unicorn_ across an adjacent closed container and then open the container. Inside, you'll find a Common [consumable](/Traits.aspx?ID=36) alchemical or magic item you need to solve a problem or deal with a situation close at hand. For example, if you're badly wounded, you might find a [_greater healing potion_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=186). If you've been poisoned, you could discover a [_panacea_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=188). If you're faced with a written clue in a language you don't understand, the container might hold a [comprehension elixir](/Equipment.aspx?ID=88). The GM always decides what consumable item is discovered, and it must be equal to or lower than your level. If the consumable isn't used within 1 minute of being discovered, the item vanishes. If there's no appropriate item to solve your situation, the GM can rule that no object is found; in this case, the daily use of _The Unicorn_ is not expended.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

_The Winged Serpent_ represents the wisdom of knowing when to act. As long as you have _The Winged Serpent_ invested, you gain a +2 item bonus on initiative checks made with sight-based Perception checks. This bonus increases to +3 if you are 17th level.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> envision, Interact;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You swipe _The Winged Serpent_ across your eyes to gain insight on when to strike. You cast [_foresight_](/Spells.aspx?ID=127) on yourself, but the duration is only 10 minutes. If you're at least 17th level, the duration extends to 1 hour and you can cast _foresight_ on another creature by touch. The level of this spell is one-half your level rounded down (minimum 9th).

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 6+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Grub Gloves](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2561)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Necromancy" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=117" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Highhelm](/Sources.aspx?ID=204) pg. 87

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn gloves

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Danila Kenn and Depa Stepwell of Common Goods developed these gloves after complaints from an exhausted grindlegrub rancher. Since their development, the gloves have become popular not just among grindlegrub ranchers, but laborers of all kinds in Stonebreach. The thick gloves are designed to help ranchers hold on to the squirming, slimy bodies of grindlegrubs, and help soothe the wear and tear of the day while restoring energy for after-hours pursuits. While wearing the gloves, you gain a +1 item bonus to [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) checks. The gripping power of the gloves provides you with a +2 circumstance bonus to Reflex saves to [Grab an Edge](/Actions.aspx?ID=95). If you roll a success to Grab an Edge, you get a critical success instead, as the _gripping gloves_ allow you to hold on even with just a few fingertips.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> envision, Interact;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You rub your hands together and take a deep breath to prepare for the rest of your day. You regain 3d8+10 Hit Points and feel refreshed, losing the [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=15) condition. As normal for effects that remove fatigue, this doesn't remove any underlying source of fatigue, such as lack of sleep, causing the condition to return if the underlying source isn't addressed.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

While wearing the gloves, you gain a +1 item bonus to [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) checks. The gripping power of the gloves provides you with a +2 circumstance bonus to Reflex saves to [Grab an Edge](/Actions.aspx?ID=95). If you roll a success to Grab an Edge, you get a critical success instead, as the _gripping gloves_ allow you to hold on even with just a few fingertips.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> envision, Interact;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You rub your hands together and take a deep breath to prepare for the rest of your day. You regain 3d8+10 Hit Points and feel refreshed, losing the [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=15) condition. As normal for effects that remove fatigue, this doesn't remove any underlying source of fatigue, such as lack of sleep, causing the condition to return if the underlying source isn't addressed.

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 3"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Aerial Cloak](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2575)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Air" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=5" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 74

**Price** 60 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn cloak

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This blue cloak is surprisingly light for its length and seems to catch wind bursts in its tail, flying out behind the wearer while cushioning falls and easing jumps. The sides of the cloak are slightly weighted, making it easy to grab ahold of when it fills with air. This cloak grants you a +1 item bonus to [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) checks to [Leap](/Actions.aspx?ID=81) and a +1 item bonus to Acrobatics checks to [Balance](/Actions.aspx?ID=28) or [Maneuver in Flight](/Actions.aspx?ID=30).

**Activate—Fall Gently** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [air](/Traits.aspx?ID=5)) **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** You're [falling](/Rules.aspx?ID=401);  **Effect** The cloak catches the air and you grab onto its edges, utilizing the draft to guide you to safety. Treat your fall as 30 feet shorter and glide to a space of your choice at the bottom of your fall, which must be within 20 feet of where you would've landed.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Aerial Cloak

item_bonus_note: Acrobatics checks to Escape
markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 10"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Jaathoom's Scarf](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2582)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Air" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=5" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 75

**Price** 900 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn belt

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This scarf is made of fine silk that's the same shade of blue as a clear, cloudless sky. The short ends are edged with a fine gold fringe that seems to sway even in still weather as though touched by invisible winds. The long edges have exquisite embroidery in threads that vary from a blue identical to the silk to the deep gray of storm clouds during winter. Wearing this scarf grants a +2 item bonus to [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12) checks to dance and to [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) checks to [Escape](/Actions.aspx?ID=79).

**Activate—Vanish** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** The scarf casts 4th-rank [_invisibility_](/Spells.aspx?ID=164) on you.

**Activate—Jaathoom's Rebuke** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32)) **Frequency** once per hour;   **Effect** You let the winds around you catch the edges of the _jaathoom's scarf_, and a jaathoom shuyookh appears with a sudden updraft. The winds force your enemies back, granting you some breathing room in battle. Each enemy in a 10-foot emanation must succeed at a DC 27 Fortitude save or be [pushed](/Rules.aspx?ID=451) 10 feet. A creature that critically fails is also knocked [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=31) after being moved. Creatures with the [air](/Traits.aspx?ID=5) trait are immune to all these effects.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Jaathoom's Scarf

item_bonus_note: Games Lore in a dice game using the item
markdown:
**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 99 **Price** 1,800 gp **Usage** held in 1 hand --- _Jabali's dice_ are two six-sided dice carved from evenly weighted stone to the specifications of a specific jabali shuyookh. The sides showing a 6 also have the name and title of the shuyookh inscribed in Petran. If you whisper the name and title during a dice game using _jabali's dice_, they bless you with a bit of luck, granting a +2 item bonus to your [Games Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) check. You can do so frequently enough to apply this bonus while [Earning Income](/Actions.aspx?ID=23) using Games Lore, but only one user at a time can do so. **Activate—Jabali's Gamble** ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You call out the shuyookh's name and title, then roll the dice. The shuyookh appears briefly to provide for your defense. Roll 2d6 to determine the effect. Represented by the GM, the shuyookh chooses any effect's specifications, benefiting you according to the shuyookh's whims.
**2–5**The shuyookh casts [_mountain resilience_](/Spells.aspx?ID=312) on you.
**6–9**The shuyookh casts [_grasping earth_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1334), but you and your allies are immune to it.
**10–11**The shuyookh casts [_wall of stone_](/Spells.aspx?ID=365). The edges can pass through creatures, which are shunted to the side of the wall opposite that edge.
**12**The shuyookh casts a DC 31 [_petrify_](/Spells.aspx?ID=123) spell on a creature hostile and in proximity to you. If no appealing target exists, you receive a 10–11 result instead.
name: Jabali's Dice

    right="Item 9"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Vital Earth](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2598)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Earth" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=55" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 100

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Life-infused soil from the [Plane of Earth](/Planes.aspx?ID=5), _vital earth_ is glittering dust you inhale that causes you to not need air or water for 24 hours. Also, for this time, your wounds close easily, like molding clay, meaning someone attempting to [Administer First Aid](/Actions.aspx?ID=54) or [Treat Wounds](/Actions.aspx?ID=57) on you doesn't need a [healer's toolkit](/Equipment.aspx?ID=25) and gains a +1 item bonus to the [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=9) check.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Vital Earth

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 7+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Ash Gown](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2599)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Fire" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=72" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 122

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn clothing

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

_Ash gowns_ are formal wear spun from smoke, ash, and char collected from the [Plane of Fire](/Planes.aspx?ID=6). Typically, they're voluminous floor-length dresses or three-piece suits, but regardless of their specific tailoring, _ash gowns_ are always an ostentatious display of wealth and loyalty to the powers of the Plane of Fire. They're exceptionally popular in the courts of the [Elemental Lords](/DeityCategories.aspx?ID=8) and among the high society of Medina Mudii'a. The gown grants you resistance 5 to fire and a +1 item bonus to [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) checks.

**Activate—Blazing Promenade** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** The ash gown ignites in a ferocious blaze, flames licking the floor and trailing behind you like a dancing cape. You Stride and make a Strike at the end of your movement. During the Stride, your flames incinerate minor obstacles in your path; you ignore non-magical [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=453), and any you move through is destroyed. Creatures that are adjacent to you at any point during your movement take 2d6 fire damage with a DC 23 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save. A creature doesn't need to attempt this save more than once, even if you move past it multiple times.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 7"
pfs="Standard"
>
Ash Gown
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 122

**Price** 350 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 11"
pfs="Standard"
>
Ash Gown (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 122

**Price** 1,300 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

The resistance to fire is 10, and the bonus to Intimidation checks is +2. You can activate a _greater ash gown_ once per 10 minutes instead of once per day. The activation's damage increases to 4d6, and its DC increases to 28.
name: Ash Gown

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 5"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Brazier of Harmony](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2601)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Censer" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=500" />
<trait label="Fire" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=72" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 122

**Price** 150 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

The _brazier of harmony_ is a circular, orb-shaped censer etched with celebrating creatures shaking hands and dancing. The brazier contains a pleasant-smelling potpourri of dried flowers and incenses, designed to create a calm atmosphere that encourages meditation, thoughtfulness, and camaraderie. While holding the lit censer, you gain a +1 item bonus to [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) checks, whether the censer is activated or not.

**Activate—Light Incense** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))**Cost** incense worth at least 1 sp; **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** When the incense is lit, pleasant, floral smoke surrounds the censer in a 20-foot emanation, creating a space of peace and harmony. Each creature that breathes the smoke is affected by 3rd-rank [_calm_](/Spells.aspx?ID=31) and is then temporarily immune for 24 hours. The spell's effects end when the incense burns out.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Brazier of Harmony

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 14"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Rhyton of the Radiant Ifrit](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2607)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Fire" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=72" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 124

**Price** 4,200 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This exquisite, shimmering rhyton is made from volcanic glass and encrusted with rubies, garnets, and other red gemstones, with a blown glass sculpture of a snakelike fire elemental at its base. It was sculpted as part of a 10-piece set of drinking horns, created to match a powerful ifrit shuyookh's entertaining dinnerware, but it became separated from the rest of the collection. Whether for good or ill, the beautiful glass rhyton still carries tidings from its previous shuyookh owner, and you become their esteemed guest from afar whenever you hold it.

 The drinking horn functions as a [bottomless stein](/Equipment.aspx?ID=563), refilling with a common ale each time it's emptied. However, anyone holding the rhyton who whispers the name of its former shuyookh owner has the horn filled with their favorite wine, spirit, juice, or other beverage from anywhere across the many planes, as if their wish were granted by a magnanimous genie.

**Activate—Toast!** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))    **Effect** You raise a toast to a creature or creatures you're about to socialize with. You gain a +2 item bonus to [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5), [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6), or [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) checks against those creatures for 1 hour.

**Activate—Ifrit's Command** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** An ifrit's hospitality always comes with an implied threat. You cause the shuyookh to briefly appear and take its vengeance on those who would hurt you, the genie's “guest.” The shuyookh issues a 6th-rank [_command_](/Spells.aspx?ID=45) that targets all creatures [hostile](/Conditions.aspx?ID=23) to you in range instead of the usual number of targets. The shuyookh issues the same command to all of them. Each target that fails its save also feels all nourishment leached from it, becoming [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=15) as long as it's affected by the _command_.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Rhyton of the Radiant Ifrit

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 3"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Smoke Veil](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2609)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Fire" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=72" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 124

**Price** 60 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn headwear

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

_Smoke veils_ are wigs or headdresses made of flame and ash, giving the wearer a burning coil of fiery hair and concealing their face behind a smoldering, omnipresent haze of smoke and sparking embers. You can use the veil to go unrecognized by hiding your face so that you can attempt [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) checks to [Impersonate](/Actions.aspx?ID=46) without needing a [disguise kit](/Equipment.aspx?ID=19). When you do so, it takes you only 1 minute to create the disguise, and you gain a +1 item bonus to the check. You still need a disguise kit and the full time if you're using cosmetics and other props to change other aspects of your disguise, or if Impersonating a specific person.

**Activate—Blazing Stare** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32))   **Requirements** You dealt fire damage to a target you can see within 30 feet with your most recent action this turn; **Effect** You set your fiery gaze on your target, eyes burning within a cloud of ash and cinder. Roll an [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) check to [Demoralize](/Actions.aspx?ID=53) the target. Demoralize loses the [auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=16) trait and gains the [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=163) trait, and you don't take a penalty when you attempt to Demoralize a creature that doesn't understand your language.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Smoke Veil

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 12"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Resonant Guitar](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2616)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Metal" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=505" />
<trait label="Sonic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=147" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 146

**Price** 1,850 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Every part of this dark, shining guitar, from the strings to the soundboard, is constructed of metal. When the strings are strummed, the gilding on the guitar ripples like a liquid. A _resonant guitar_ is a [virtuoso handheld musical instrument](/Equipment.aspx?ID=37) that grants a +2 item bonus to [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12) checks attempted while using it.

 A _resonant guitar_ is a planar key for [_interplanar teleport_](/Spells.aspx?ID=222) and similar magic to travel to the [Plane of Metal](/Planes.aspx?ID=24). When using it in this way, you can play a tune inspired by your destination to make it more likely to arrive where you intend to be, appearing 1d6×25 miles from your intended destination instead of 1d10×25 miles away.

**Activate—Strum of Thunder** <actions string="" /> 1 minute ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You play a magnetic tune, enchanting one metallic weapon within 60 feet of you. This item gains the [_thundering_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=306) rune for 1 hour.

**Activate—Chord of Protection** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** A creature within 30 feet of you targets you or an ally with a melee attack;  **Effect** You strike a piercing chord, putting up an invisible sound barrier between the target and the attacker. The target gains a +2 status bonus to AC against the triggering attack. If the Strike still hits, the barrier breaks, dealing 3d10 sonic damage to the attacker.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Resonant Guitar

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 12"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Conch of Otherworldly Seas](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2626)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Water" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=165" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 176

**Price** 1,850 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Magical writing covers the surface of this beautiful conch shell, which emits a blue light from inside. A _conch of otherworldly seas_ is a [virtuoso handheld musical instrument](/Equipment.aspx?ID=37) that grants a +2 item bonus to [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12) checks attempted while using it. 

The conch is a planar key for [_interplanar teleport_](/Spells.aspx?ID=222) and similar magic to travel to the [Plane of Water](/Planes.aspx?ID=7). When using it this way, you can attune it to the waters of your destination to make it more likely to arrive where you intend to be, appearing 1d6×25 miles from your intended destination instead of 1d10×25 miles away.

**Activate—Voice of Oceans** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))    **Effect** You hold the horn to your ear and can understand and speak [Thalassic](/Languages.aspx?ID=113) as long as it remains there.

**Activate—Sounds of the Deep** <actions string="" /> 10 minutes ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))    **Effect** You hold the horn to your ear and touch the correct series of runes inscribed on its surface, causing the conch to cast a 5th-rank [_clairaudience_](/Spells.aspx?ID=39) spell for your benefit. Provided you choose a location that's underwater, you can extend the spell's range to 1 mile and hear with perfect clarity.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Conch of Otherworldly Seas

While holding the spear, you gain a +2 item bonus to [Athletic](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) checks to swim.

**Activate—Lionfish Poison** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32)) **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** You successfully Strike a creature with the _lionfish spear_;  **Effect** The spear injects lionfish poison into the target; **Saving Throw** DC 28 Fortitude; **Maximum Duration** 6 rounds; **Stage 1** 3d6 poison damage and [sickened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34) (1 round); **Stage 2** 5d6 poison damage and sickened 2 (1 round); **Stage 3** [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28) for 2d4 hours
