>
[Sharkskin Robe](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2631)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Water" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=165" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 178

**Price** 1,900 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn clothing

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This sandy-textured robe comes with sleeves that resemble dorsal fins. It's believed to have been developed by frustrated alchemists from the [Universe](/Planes.aspx?ID=1) for trips to the [Plane of Water](/Planes.aspx?ID=7). The sharkskin robe grants you a swim Speed equal to your land Speed and a +2 item bonus to [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) checks.

**Activate—Shark's Elegance** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) **Frequency** once per hour;   **Effect** For 1 minute, any time you make a Strike, your weapon or [unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=199) attack gains the benefit of the [_underwater_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2634) weapon property rune.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Sharkskin Robe

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 11+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Carver-Cutter](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2640)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Wood" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=508" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 201

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Weapon** [Battle Axe](/Weapons.aspx?ID=16)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Many of the experienced woodcarvers of the [Plane of Wood](/Planes.aspx?ID=25) learn to fight as well, the better to travel into hazardous areas in search of rare and beautiful types of wood for their craft. A _carver-cutter_ serves a dual role as a weapon and a tool for felling trees and engraving wood. The _+2 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [battle axe](/Weapons.aspx?ID=16)_ looks like an exquisitely made woodcutting axe.

**Activate—Carving Chisel** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))    **Effect** You pull free a woodworking chisel stored in the knob of the axe. It can have any standard chisel shape you imagine as you activate this ability. You get a +2 item bonus to [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4) checks for woodworking if you use chisels from the _carver-cutter_. The chisel also functions as a [main-gauche](/Weapons.aspx?ID=32). All runes on the axe are replicated on the chisel, but only while both items are on the same person. You can Interact to reinsert the chisel.

**Activate—Chop Down** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ;  **Frequency** once per hour;   **Effect** Make a Strike with the axe against a [plant](/Traits.aspx?ID=125) creature or a creature made primarily of wood. This Strike deals an additional 2d6 precision damage, and on a hit, the creature is [clumsy 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=3) for 1 minute.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

</traits>

All runes on the axe are replicated on the chisel, but only while both items are on the same person. You can Interact to reinsert the chisel.

**Activate—Chop Down** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ;  **Frequency** once per hour;   **Effect** Make a Strike with the axe against a [plant](/Traits.aspx?ID=125) creature or a creature made primarily of wood. This Strike deals an additional 2d6 precision damage, and on a hit, the creature is [clumsy 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=3) for 1 minute.

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 10+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Tales in Timber](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2648)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Wood" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=508" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 202

**Price** 850 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn armor

**Bulk** 2
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Armor** [Wooden Breastplate](/Armor.aspx?ID=36)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Dozens of pictorial stories—told in wood carvings, painting, or pyrography—adorn every outer surface of this _+1 [resilient](/Equipment.aspx?ID=279) [wooden breastplate](/Armor.aspx?ID=36)_. All are old, and many truly ancient, depicting tales set eons ago on the [Plane of Wood](/Planes.aspx?ID=25). You gain a +2 item bonus to [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) checks to [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=26). If you know the [_collective memories_](/Rituals.aspx?ID=15) ritual, you can use Nature instead of [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) if you're the primary caster.

**Activate—Statue Disguise** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** The armor makes you look like a wooden statue version of one of the creatures depicted on the armor for 1 hour. This is a 3rd-rank [_illusory disguise_](/Spells.aspx?ID=159), but it can make you look like any creature depicted on the armor. This doesn't change your size or the capabilities of your body, and it scales the appearance of the creature to match yours. You can Dismiss the activation. This disguise is just real enough to make you somewhat closer to a creature of wood. You're affected by two spells while it lasts: [_oaken resilience_](/Spells.aspx?ID=20) and [_speak with plants_](/Spells.aspx?ID=294). If you stand still, you gain a +2 item bonus to [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) checks and DCs to appear as an inanimate wooden statue, in addition to the status bonus to Deception from _illusory disguise_.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 10"
pfs="Standard"
>
Tales in Timber
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 202

**Price** 1,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** 2

</row>

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 13"
pfs="Standard"
>
Tales in Timber (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 202

**Price** 2,800 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** 2

</row>

</column>

---

The armor is _+2 resilient wooden breastplate_, and the _oaken resilience_ is 4th rank.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 18"
pfs="Standard"
>
Tales in Timber (Major)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 202

**Price** 22,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** 2

</row>

</column>

---

The armor is _+2 greater resilient wooden breastplate_, the item bonus to Nature checks is +3, and the _oaken resilience_ is 6th rank.
name: Tales in Timber

item_bonus_note: Item activation, Deception checks and DCs to appear as an inanimate wooden statue
markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 10+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Tales in Timber](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2648)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Wood" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=508" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 202

**Price** 850 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn armor

**Bulk** 2
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Armor** [Wooden Breastplate](/Armor.aspx?ID=36)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Dozens of pictorial stories—told in wood carvings, painting, or pyrography—adorn every outer surface of this _+1 [resilient](/Equipment.aspx?ID=279) [wooden breastplate](/Armor.aspx?ID=36)_. All are old, and many truly ancient, depicting tales set eons ago on the [Plane of Wood](/Planes.aspx?ID=25). You gain a +2 item bonus to [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) checks to [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=26). If you know the [_collective memories_](/Rituals.aspx?ID=15) ritual, you can use Nature instead of [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) if you're the primary caster.

**Activate—Statue Disguise** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** The armor makes you look like a wooden statue version of one of the creatures depicted on the armor for 1 hour. This is a 3rd-rank [_illusory disguise_](/Spells.aspx?ID=159), but it can make you look like any creature depicted on the armor. This doesn't change your size or the capabilities of your body, and it scales the appearance of the creature to match yours. You can Dismiss the activation. This disguise is just real enough to make you somewhat closer to a creature of wood. You're affected by two spells while it lasts: [_oaken resilience_](/Spells.aspx?ID=20) and [_speak with plants_](/Spells.aspx?ID=294). If you stand still, you gain a +2 item bonus to [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) checks and DCs to appear as an inanimate wooden statue, in addition to the status bonus to Deception from _illusory disguise_.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

item_bonus_note: visual Perception checks
markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 9"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Jann's Prism](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2655)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 224

**Price** 700 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Light always seems to be refracting through this simple prism, creating a hazy multicolor aura that surrounds the glass. When exposed to direct sunlight, the prism radiates a beam of light that shifts in color. This beam spells out the name of the jann shuyookh for whom the prism was designed. While holding the prism to your eye, your vision becomes overwhelmed with colors that guide your eye, granting you a +2 item bonus to visual Perception checks. If you look through the prism while you Seek, you can scan or search an area twice as large as normal (a 60-foot cone, 30-foot burst, or 20-foot square) as the varying colors help you distinguish between your surroundings.

**Activate—Jann's Light** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You raise the prism above your head and call upon the jann shuyookh to come to your aid. The shuyookh's face becomes visible in a reflection in the prism and light shines out from the prism, surrounding you in a multitude of colors. For 1 minute, you shed [bright light](/Rules.aspx?ID=407) in a 20-foot emanation (and [dim light](/Rules.aspx?ID=408) for the next 20 feet). The light coruscates with two colors chosen by the jann, and you gain resistance 5 to two damage types based on the colors chosen: **red** [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72), **orange** [acid](/Traits.aspx?ID=3), **yellow** [electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=56), **green** [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), **blue** [sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=147), **indigo** [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), or **violet** [force](/Traits.aspx?ID=75).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Jann's Prism

item_bonus_note: Demoralize a specific creature
markdown:
May contain spoilers from Season of Ghosts **Source** [Pathfinder #196: The Summer That Never Was](/Sources.aspx?ID=214) pg. 81 **Price** 100 gp **Usage** worn cloak **Bulk** L --- While [Yaezhing](/Deities.aspx?ID=184) is feared and seldom worshipped in the open, some regions of Tian Xia see him as a god of necessary evil and their only hope for justice. This garment is often worn by bounty hunters or priests of Yaezhing, yet non-worshippers of the god of harsh justice sometimes wear this item without fear of religious persecution. The red cape appears almost black while in the shadows, with a lighter red mandala pattern on it that resembles a shuriken. When you invest a _cape of justice_, you must designate a single creature as your offender. You must have met the creature before, or the creature should have a persona well known to the public (for instance, a bandit's epithet, if not their face as shown on a wanted poster). You gain a +1 item bonus to [Demoralize](/Actions.aspx?ID=53) this creature. If you don't designate a creature as your offender when you invest the _cape of justice_, or if 24 hours pass without you either slaying or capturing your offender, your thoughts become worried and distracted and you take a –1 item penalty to Perception checks for the next 24 hours. **Activate** command, Interact ([force](/Traits.aspx?ID=75)) **Frequency** once per hour; **Effect** By using a free hand to grab the edge of your cape and give it a quick flourish, you cause a red, shuriken-shaped bolt of force to fire at a target within 60 feet that you can see. The shuriken hits automatically and deals 1d4+1 force damage, or 2d4+2 force damage if the target is your designated offender. If the target is within 30 feet, you can attempt to Demoralize the target as a free action after the shuriken strikes them. **Activate** command, Interact ([incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=93)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You produce [manacles](/Equipment.aspx?ID=32) from the cape and then fling them at a Medium or Small bipedal target within 30 feet. The target must attempt a DC 18 Reflex save. **Critical Success** The target is unaffected, and the manacles vanish.
**Success** The manacles strike the target's legs but fail to latch properly. The target is [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) until the start of your next turn, at which point the manacles vanish.
**Failure** The manacles lock on the target's legs, causing the target to take a –15-foot circumstance penalty to its Speeds for 1 minute, or until it [Escapes](/Actions.aspx?ID=79) or is freed by someone using [Pick a Lock](/Actions.aspx?ID=70) (DC 18), after which the manacles vanish.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but the target is [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=24) for as long as the manacles remain in place.
name: Cape of Justice

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 3"
    pfs=""
>
[Hongrui's Gratitude](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2684)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Season of Ghosts</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Unique" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=161" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #196: The Summer That Never Was](/Sources.aspx?ID=214) pg. 82

**Price** 60 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn cloak

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Given as thanks for honoring the memories of three unfortunate travelers who met a tragic end in the Willowshore Hinterlands, this red-hooded cloak is decorated with an image of an elaborately dressed performer whose two companions hold a lantern and umbrella for her.

**Activate** <actions string="Three Actions" /> command, envision, Interact ([evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65), [light](/Traits.aspx?ID=100)) **Frequency** once per day;  **Requirements** You don't have the umbrella activation of this cloak active; **Effect** With a wave of the cloak to the left, you cause the image of the lantern-holding companion on the cloak to vanish. A floating red lantern appears at your side and follows you at an arm's length while casting [bright light](/Rules.aspx?ID=407) in a 20-foot radius (and [dim light](/Rules.aspx?ID=408) for the next 20 feet) like a [torch](/Equipment.aspx?ID=60). For 1 hour, while the light from this lantern is active, you gain a +1 item bonus to [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) checks. You can Activate the cloak again to dismiss the torch.

**Activate** <actions string="Three Actions" /> command, envision, Interact ([abjuration](/Traits.aspx?ID=2)) **Frequency** once per day;  **Requirements** You don't have the lantern activation of this cloak active; **Effect** With a wave of the cloak to the right, you cause the image of the umbrella-holding companion on the cloak to vanish. An indestructible red wax paper umbrella appears above you and follows you, shielding you from weather effects, such as rain or bright sunlight. For 1 hour, while this umbrella is active, you gain a +1 item bonus to [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) checks. You can Activate the cloak again to dismiss the umbrella.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Hongrui's Gratitude

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 3"
    pfs=""
>
[Spider Lily Tattoo](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2687)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Season of Ghosts</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Necromancy" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=117" />
<trait label="Tattoo" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=262" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #196: The Summer That Never Was](/Sources.aspx?ID=214) pg. 82

**Price** 60 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** tattooed on the body

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

The _spider lily tattoo_ marks you as a trusted member of Granny Hu's network. This crimson tattoo fades and becomes invisible within a day of being applied, reappearing only when you Activate it, when you gain the [doomed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=9) condition, or when you die. The higher the doomed value, the more vivid the color. If your tattoo is plainly visible, you gain a +1 item bonus to [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) checks against all creatures that can see the tattoo, but you take a –1 item penalty to [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) checks to [Make an Impression](/Actions.aspx?ID=50) on those who understand the actual meaning of the _spider lily tattoo_ (including all of Willowshore's citizens).

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> envision ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32))   **Requirements** Your tattoo isn't currently visible; **Effect** You make the tattoo visible. You can use this activation again to make the tattoo not visible, unless you have the doomed condition.

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> envision ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32)) **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** An [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=160) creature detects you for the first time;  **Effect** The _spider lily tattoo_ manipulates your life force to make you appear to be undead for a short time. Attempt a [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) check against the triggering undead creature's Perception DC. On a success, the triggering undead believes you're undead as well—a [mindless](/Traits.aspx?ID=108) undead is likely to ignore you, while a sapient undead might react with curiosity or confusion. You can continue attempting Deception checks each round as a single action to Sustain the effect for up to 1 minute.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Spider Lily Tattoo

>
[Spider Lily Tattoo](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2687)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Season of Ghosts</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Necromancy" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=117" />
<trait label="Tattoo" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=262" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #196: The Summer That Never Was](/Sources.aspx?ID=214) pg. 82

**Price** 60 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** tattooed on the body

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

The _spider lily tattoo_ marks you as a trusted member of Granny Hu's network. This crimson tattoo fades and becomes invisible within a day of being applied, reappearing only when you Activate it, when you gain the [doomed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=9) condition, or when you die. The higher the doomed value, the more vivid the color. If your tattoo is plainly visible, you gain a +1 item bonus to [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) checks against all creatures that can see the tattoo, but you take a –1 item penalty to [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) checks to [Make an Impression](/Actions.aspx?ID=50) on those who understand the actual meaning of the _spider lily tattoo_ (including all of Willowshore's citizens).

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> envision ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32))   **Requirements** Your tattoo isn't currently visible; **Effect** You make the tattoo visible. You can use this activation again to make the tattoo not visible, unless you have the doomed condition.

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> envision ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32)) **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** An [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=160) creature detects you for the first time;  **Effect** The _spider lily tattoo_ manipulates your life force to make you appear to be undead for a short time. Attempt a [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) check against the triggering undead creature's Perception DC. On a success, the triggering undead believes you're undead as well—a [mindless](/Traits.aspx?ID=108) undead is likely to ignore you, while a sapient undead might react with curiosity or confusion. You can continue attempting Deception checks each round as a single action to Sustain the effect for up to 1 minute.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Spider Lily Tattoo

[Privacy Ward Fulu](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2692)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Season of Ghosts</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Abjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=2" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Fulu" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=378" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #196: The Summer That Never Was](/Sources.aspx?ID=214) pg. 83

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** affixed to wall

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This fulu seeks to keep thieves, spies, and unwanted attention away from a room. A depiction of a lock appears in the center of this fulu, which is in turn surrounded by circles of broken keys. When applied to a wall inside a room, all creatures within the room gain an item bonus to [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) checks against creatures outside the room.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 1"
pfs="Standard"
>
Privacy Ward Fulu (Hallway)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #196: The Summer That Never Was](/Sources.aspx?ID=214) pg. 83

**Price** 4 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The _privacy ward fulu_ can shield a room of up to 10 square feet, grants a +1 item bonus, and has a duration of 1 hour.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 5"
pfs="Standard"
>
Privacy Ward Fulu (Room)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #196: The Summer That Never Was](/Sources.aspx?ID=214) pg. 83

**Price** 25 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The fulu can shield a room of up to 30 square feet, grants a +1 item bonus, and has a duration of 8 hours. If you become [hidden](/Conditions.aspx?ID=22) within the room, you automatically become [undetected](/Conditions.aspx?ID=39) to creatures of the fulu's level or lower.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 9"
pfs="Standard"
>
Privacy Ward Fulu (Chamber)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #196: The Summer That Never Was](/Sources.aspx?ID=214) pg. 83

**Price** 120 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The fulu can shield a room of up to 90 square feet, grants a +2 item bonus, and has a duration of 24 hours. If you become hidden within the room, you automatically become undetected to creatures of the fulu's level or lower.

---

Handheld instruments include bagpipes, a small set of chimes, small drums, fiddles and viols, flutes and recorders, small harps, lutes, trumpets, and similarly sized instruments. The GM might rule that an especially large handheld instrument (like a tuba) has greater Bulk. Heavy instruments such as large drums, a full set of chimes, and keyboard instruments are less portable and generally need to be stationary while being played.

 A virtuoso instrument is more finely made and gives a +1 item bonus to [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12) checks using that instrument.

---

Handheld instruments include bagpipes, a small set of chimes, small drums, fiddles and viols, flutes and recorders, small harps, lutes, trumpets, and similarly sized instruments. The GM might rule that an especially large handheld instrument (like a tuba) has greater Bulk. Heavy instruments such as large drums, a full set of chimes, and keyboard instruments are less portable and generally need to be stationary while being played.

 A virtuoso instrument is more finely made and gives a +1 item bonus to [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12) checks using that instrument.
